vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当短跑运动员转弯时，有两种重要的力量在发挥作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 弥补, 添字首音
vocabulary.context: Research now shows that even which leg the prosthesis is on can affect how fast someone can take a curve.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在的研究表明，即使假肢安装在哪条腿上也会影响一个人转弯的速度

vocabulary.definition: n. 被截肢者；[医] 切肢者
vocabulary.context: The scientists compared Paralympic sprinters who had amputations above the ankle to non-amputee runners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家们将脚踝以上截肢的残奥短跑运动员与未截肢的跑步运动员进行了比较

vocabulary.definition: adj. 向心的；[医] 向心的, 向中的, 求中的
vocabulary.context: But centripetal (Sen-TRIP-eh-tul) force also is at work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但向心力 (Sen-TRIP-eh-tul) 也在发挥作用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 顺时针方向的；adv. 顺时针方向地
vocabulary.context: They also looked at each as they ran clockwise and counterclockwise around a track.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当他们绕着轨道顺时针和逆时针奔跑时，他们还观察了每个人

vocabulary.definition: n. 刹车, 阻碍, 丛林；v. 刹车
vocabulary.context: Curves tend to put the brakes on human runners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 曲线往往会给人类跑步者带来刹车

vocabulary.definition: v. 偿还, 补偿, 付报酬
vocabulary.context: So they couldn't compensate as well for their artificial limb generating less force against the ground compared to a regular ankle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，与普通脚踝相比，他们无法补偿假肢对地面产生的力较小

vocabulary.definition: n. 缺点, 不利, 坏处
vocabulary.context: Left-leg amputees therefore may be at a disadvantage, the data indicate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 数据表明，左腿截肢者可能处于不利地位

vocabulary.definition: adj. 实验的, 根据实验的；[医] 实验的
vocabulary.context: The team published its results in the March 16 issue of the Journal of Experimental Biology .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该团队在3月16日的《实验生物学杂志》上发表了其研究结果

vocabulary.definition: n. a. 【棒】中锋；[网络] 台发电机可并联
vocabulary.context: When Royals centerfielder Lorenzo Cain stepped up to the plate at the bottom of the ninth, things looked grim.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当皇家队中场球员洛伦佐·凯恩在第九局下半站上本垒时，事情看起来很严峻

vocabulary.definition: n. [棒](投手投出的)不旋转球
vocabulary.context: Nathan is currently researching the knuckleball.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 内森目前正在研究指关节球

vocabulary.definition: n. (球,轮等的)逆转, 倒旋
vocabulary.context: In a 2008 paper published in the American Journal of Physics , for example, Nathan found that doubling the backspin on a ball caused it to stay in the air longer, fly higher, and sail farther.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，内森在 2008 年发表在《美国物理学杂志》上的一篇论文中发现，使球的后旋加倍可以使其在空中停留的时间更长、飞得更高、飞得更远

vocabulary.definition: adj. 瞬间发生的, 刹那间发生的
vocabulary.context: Success or failure, for a batter, comes down to something that happens in a split-second: The collision between a bat and the ball.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于击球手来说，成功或失败取决于瞬间发生的事情：球棒和球之间的碰撞

vocabulary.definition: n. 进行劈(切、割)的人, 劈裂机, 分离机, 分解器；[化] 分流器; 导流板; 片皮机
vocabulary.context: In the Royals game against the Tigers, Detroit pitcher Valverde threw a splitter, the nickname for a split-finger fastball, against Cain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在皇家队对阵老虎队的比赛中，底特律投手巴尔韦德向凯恩投出了一记分叉球（分叉快球的昵称）

vocabulary.definition: n. 棒球运动( baseball的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: Its researchers use a cannon to fire baseballs at bats in a box outfitted with devices that then measure the speed and direction of each ball.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其研究人员使用大炮向装有装置的盒子中的球棒发射棒球，然后测量每个球的速度和方向

vocabulary.definition: n. 粗糙, 凹凸不平, 粗糙度, 篷乱毛发；[化] 粗糙度; 不平度
vocabulary.context: This roughness may change how much a ball will be slowed by drag.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种粗糙度可能会改变球因阻力而减慢的程度

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可预言地
vocabulary.context: Instead, natural laws are descriptions of the way nature behaves, both invariably and predictably.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，自然法则是对自然行为方式的描述，既是不变的又是可预测的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非决定性的, 犹豫不决的, 不明确的
vocabulary.context: It may fly this way and that, as if it were indecisive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它可能会飞来飞去，好像优柔寡断

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可测量的, 不久的, 重要的
vocabulary.context: It links a measurable share of that warming to human activities that release CO2.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它将变暖的很大一部分与释放二氧化碳的人类活动联系起来

vocabulary.definition: abbr. 超文本传输协议安全（Hyper Text Transfer Protocol）
vocabulary.context: For rights to republish Science News for Students articles in assessments, course packs, or textbooks, visit: https://www.societyforscience.org/permission-republish.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如需重新发布评估、课程包或教科书中的学生科学新闻文章的权利，请访问：https://www.societyforscience.org/permission-republish

vocabulary.definition: n. 瓦(特)；[化] 瓦; 瓦特
vocabulary.context: After sifting through these data, the team showed that a rise in CO2 levels of 22 parts per million in air boosted the amount of the sun's heat on the ground by 0.2 watts per square meter.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在筛选这些数据后，研究小组发现，空气中二氧化碳浓度上升 22 ppm 会使地面上的太阳热量增加每平方米 0.2 瓦

vocabulary.definition: n. 波长；[化] 波长
vocabulary.context: CO2 absorbs some wavelengths of the infrared light now being radiated from Earth's surface.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 二氧化碳吸收地球表面辐射的某些波长的红外光

vocabulary.definition: n. 发展, 集结, 增强
vocabulary.context: Knowing this, the researchers could look at the wavelengths of infrared light and, like a fingerprint, link it to what share was from a CO2 buildup in the air, and what share was due to other things, such as water vapor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 知道这一点后，研究人员可以观察红外光的波长，并像指纹一样，将其与空气中二氧化碳积聚的份额以及水蒸气等其他物质的份额联系起来

vocabulary.definition: n. 指纹；v.t. 采指纹
vocabulary.context: Knowing this, the researchers could look at the wavelengths of infrared light and, like a fingerprint, link it to what share was from a CO2 buildup in the air, and what share was due to other things, such as water vapor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 知道这一点后，研究人员可以观察红外光的波长，并像指纹一样，将其与空气中二氧化碳积聚的份额以及水蒸气等其他物质的份额联系起来

vocabulary.definition: n. 警棍, 伙伴
vocabulary.context: By that, "Pollution" by Billy Wilson is licensed under CC scientists mean that this gas allows the sun's BY-NC 2.0.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由此可见，Billy Wilson 的"污染"已获得 CC 科学家的许可，这意味着这种气体允许太阳的 BY-NC 2.0

vocabulary.definition: n. 辐射；[化] 放射
vocabulary.context: This infrared radiation goes in all directions - including back to Earth's surface.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种红外辐射向各个方向传播--包括返回地球表面

vocabulary.definition: n. 研究人员
vocabulary.context: Knowing this, the researchers could look at the wavelengths of infrared light and, like a fingerprint, link it to what share was from a CO2 buildup in the air, and what share was due to other things, such as water vapor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 知道这一点后，研究人员可以观察红外光的波长，并像指纹一样，将其与空气中二氧化碳积聚的份额以及水蒸气等其他物质的份额联系起来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 原则的, 有原则的, 原则性的
vocabulary.context: Goodness will prevail in the end and we just need to keep doing the right[,] principled thing to always have the moral high ground[.]
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 善良最终会占上风，我们只需要继续做正确的事情[,]有原则的事情就可以永远占据道德制高点[.]

vocabulary.definition: adj. 累积的；[医] 蓄积的, 累积的
vocabulary.context: All these things had a cumulative effect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所有这些都产生了累积效应

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高贵的；华丽的
vocabulary.context: If they're going to hit us and hurt us, there couldn't be anything more nobler than to stand up for human rights and civil rights at that time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果他们要打我们、伤害我们，当时没有什么比挺身而出维护人权和公民权利更高尚的了

vocabulary.definition: n. 廉正, 正直, 公正, 正确性
vocabulary.context: We see the influence of nonviolence and rightness going on for years afterwards.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们看到非暴力和正义的影响在之后的许多年里持续存在

vocabulary.definition: n. 枪的表尺, 事后聪明
vocabulary.context: This, what I will call a movement, it turned out in hindsight to be a movement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这，我称之为运动，事后证明它是一场运动

vocabulary.definition: adj. 各式各样的；[经] 杂的, 各种, 各式各样的
vocabulary.context: We were, previously, in other parts of the store where we purchased items like toothpaste, notebook paper, other sundry items, without incident.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此前，我们在商店的其他地方购买了牙膏、笔记本纸和其他杂货，没有发生任何事故

vocabulary.definition: n. 片段, 部分, 分节, 段；v. 分割, 分裂；[计] 段
vocabulary.context: People from every segment of life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 来自生活各个领域的人们

vocabulary.definition: n. 上下文, 背景, 来龙去脉；n. 上下文；[计] 上下文
vocabulary.context: It all has to be put in context.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一切都必须放在上下文中

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未切成片的（面包）
vocabulary.context: The houses on that block in Queens, New York, were attached to one another like an unsliced loaf of bread, and our little brown sliver was indistinguishable from the rest.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 纽约皇后区那个街区的房子像一块未切片的面包一样连在一起，而我们的小棕色条子与其他房子没有什么区别

vocabulary.definition: n. 建筑物的正面, 外观, 表面
vocabulary.context: The woman next to her, the oldest resident in Woodside, praised our family for preserving the Tudor facade on our home.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她旁边的那位女士是伍德赛德最年长的居民，她称赞我们家保留了都铎王朝的外观

vocabulary.definition: v. 重新集合, 重新召集( reconvene的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: Our conversations abruptly ended, but so did my father's peace of mind once he saw that we reconvened on the front porch.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的谈话突然结束了，但当我父亲看到我们在前廊再次见面时，他的心也平静了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有限的, 有穷的, 限定的；n. 有限物
vocabulary.context: It's my turn to ask questions, so I say to her, "Do you ever dream of exploring our one finite world?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 轮到我提问了，所以我对她说："你有没有梦想过探索我们这个有限的世界？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有关建筑的, 建筑学的
vocabulary.context: a type of architectural design that was popular from 1890-1940 5.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1890-1940年流行的一种建筑设计类型 5.

vocabulary.definition: n. 呈齿状起伏的山脉
vocabulary.context: Originally published in Sierra Magazine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最初发表于塞拉杂志

vocabulary.definition: adj. 听不见的
vocabulary.context: Like a dog whistle that can't be heard by human ears, the planes were inaudible to me.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就像人耳听不到的狗哨一样，我听不到飞机的声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 市郊住宅区, 郊区, 边缘
vocabulary.context: The people I knew who had grown up in suburbs and summer homes bought my story, I suppose, because it was consistent with a narrative we'd all learned from the movies and the stories that charities told about city kids, especially ones like me, the ones who weren't white.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我想，我认识的那些在郊区和避暑别墅长大的人都买了我的故事，因为它与我们从电影和慈善机构讲述的城市孩子的故事中学到的叙述一致，尤其是像我这样的非白人孩子

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 剥夺, 使丧失；[法] 剥夺, 剥夺, 夺去
vocabulary.context: Yes, I assured them, I had grown up deprived.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是的，我向他们保证，我是在贫困中长大的

vocabulary.definition: n. （足球和一些其他体育运动中的）队员兼主教练
vocabulary.context: Cuban native José Méndez, considered one of the best pitchers of his era in any league, led the Kansas City Monarchs to multiple championships in the mid-1920s as the team's player-manager.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古巴人何塞·门德斯 (José Méndez) 被认为是他那个时代在任何联盟中最好的投手之一，他在 20 年代中期作为球队的球员兼教练带领堪萨斯城君主队多次夺得冠军

vocabulary.definition: n. 安排演出的窍门, 吸引观众的窍门, 经营术, 招徕生意的手腕
vocabulary.context: The legendary pitcher Satchel Paige, who was as famous for his showmanship as for his blistering fastballs, was a star on the Dominican league's Dragones as well as in the Negro Leagues and MLB, where he was the first African American to pitch in a World Series.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 传奇投手萨切尔·佩奇 (Satchel Paige) 以其出色的表演技巧和快速的快球而闻名，他是多米尼加联盟 Dragones 以及黑人联盟和美国职业棒球大联盟 (MLB) 的明星，他是第一位在世界职业棒球大赛中投球的非裔美国人

vocabulary.definition: v. 在小城镇巡回演出, 在乡间作演说旅行
vocabulary.context: Multiracial Cuban teams barnstormed through the United States, playing pick-up exhibition games against semi-pro teams, amateurs, local clubs, and other barnstormers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 多种族的古巴球队在美国巡回演出，与半职业球队、业余爱好者、当地俱乐部和其他巡回演出者进行临时表演赛

vocabulary.definition: n. 偷取者, 偷干者, 僭据者；[法] 小偷
vocabulary.context: Hall-of-Famer Monte Irvin, whose all-around brilliance as a power hitter, shortstop and base stealer made him one of the biggest stars in the Negro Leagues and then the MLB, said he had never felt as free as he did in 1942, the year he played for Mexico's league.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 名人堂成员蒙特·欧文（Monte Irvin）作为强力击球手、游击手和盗垒手的全能才华使他成为黑人联盟和美国职业棒球大联盟最伟大的明星之一，他说他从未像 1942 年那样感到自由，那一年他为墨西哥联盟效力

vocabulary.definition: adj. 多民族的
vocabulary.context: Multiracial Cuban teams barnstormed through the United States, playing pick-up exhibition games against semi-pro teams, amateurs, local clubs, and other barnstormers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 多种族的古巴球队在美国巡回演出，与半职业球队、业余爱好者、当地俱乐部和其他巡回演出者进行临时表演赛

vocabulary.definition: n. 游击手, 停显液
vocabulary.context: Hall-of-Famer Monte Irvin, whose all-around brilliance as a power hitter, shortstop and base stealer made him one of the biggest stars in the Negro Leagues and then the MLB, said he had never felt as free as he did in 1942, the year he played for Mexico's league.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 名人堂成员蒙特·欧文（Monte Irvin）作为强力击球手、游击手和盗垒手的全能才华使他成为黑人联盟和美国职业棒球大联盟最伟大的明星之一，他说他从未像 1942 年那样感到自由，那一年他为墨西哥联盟效力

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 入庙祀奉, 铭记
vocabulary.context: He was the first Latin American player enshrined as a Negro Leaguer in the National Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, New York.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是第一位被列为纽约库珀斯敦国家棒球名人堂的黑人联盟球员的拉丁美洲球员

vocabulary.definition: n. 倍数, 并联；adj. 多样的, 许多的, 多功能的
vocabulary.context: Cuban native José Méndez, considered one of the best pitchers of his era in any league, led the Kansas City Monarchs to multiple championships in the mid-1920s as the team's player-manager.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 古巴人何塞·门德斯 (José Méndez) 被认为是他那个时代在任何联盟中最好的投手之一，他在 20 年代中期作为球队的球员兼教练带领堪萨斯城君主队多次夺得冠军

vocabulary.definition: adj. 冷淡的, 有支架的；n. 冷淡, 平衡, 和局
vocabulary.context: Force had finally brought an end to the standoff between North and South.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 武力终于结束了南北之间的对峙

vocabulary.definition: n. 脱离, 分离
vocabulary.context: Virginia seceded from the United States and joined the Confederacy rather than send volunteers to fight against "her sister states." Three other Southern slave states quickly moved toward secession.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弗吉尼亚脱离美国并加入联邦，而不是派遣志愿者去对抗"她的姐妹州"。另外三个南方蓄奴州迅速走向分裂

vocabulary.definition: adj. 震耳欲聋的
vocabulary.context: The noise was deafening, and thick smoke made it hard for the defenders of the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 噪音震耳欲聋，浓烟让美军守军举步维艰

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 炮击, 攻击, 轰击；n. 射石炮
vocabulary.context: Confederate leaders decided to bombard the fort unless the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除非美国同意，否则南方邦联领导人决定轰炸该堡垒

vocabulary.definition: n. 炮击；[化] 轰击
vocabulary.context: The bombardment of Fort Sumter was the first battle of the American Civil War.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 轰炸萨姆特堡是美国内战的第一场战役

vocabulary.definition: n. 城堡, 要塞；v.t. 筑要塞, 以要塞防守
vocabulary.context: For 34 hours, Confederate cannon fire from the shores of South Carolina's Charleston Harbor battered the towering walls of the island fortress as fiery shells crashed into the open area in its center.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在长达 34 小时的时间里，南卡罗来纳州查尔斯顿港沿岸的南方邦联炮火猛烈地袭击了岛屿堡垒的高耸墙壁，炽热的炮弹落入其中心的空地

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 正式脱离(或退出), 分离；[法] 退出, 脱离
vocabulary.context: volunteers answering the president's call passed through on their way to Washington - increasing Lincoln's concern that Maryland, too, might secede.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 响应总统号召的志愿者在前往华盛顿的途中经过这里，这增加了林肯对马里兰州也可能脱离联邦的担忧

vocabulary.definition: n. 防卫者, 防护者, 辩护者；[法] 辩护人, 保护人
vocabulary.context: The noise was deafening, and thick smoke made it hard for the defenders of the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 噪音震耳欲聋，浓烟让美军守军举步维艰

vocabulary.definition: n. 雇用；v.t. 雇用, 使用, 使从事于
vocabulary.context: Just 82 soldiers - including members of the military band - aided by about 40 workmen employed at the fort.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只有 82 名士兵--包括军乐队成员--在堡垒雇用的大约 40 名工人的帮助下

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不惹人注意的, 给人印象不深的
vocabulary.context: The home itself was unimpressive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 房子本身并不起眼

vocabulary.definition: n. 观光客
vocabulary.context: When the news of the tree's discovery leaked out, quite a few sightseers stopped by to have a look at it, and the local government, realizing the money-making potential, began charging admission and advertising the place.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当发现这棵树的消息传出后，不少游客驻足观赏，当地政府也意识到了这棵树的赚钱潜力，开始收取入场费并进行广告宣传

vocabulary.definition: adj. 旧式的, 老派的
vocabulary.context: The O'Brien home was one of the few examples of old-style wooden structures that hadn't been demolished in Boston's urban-renewal campaign at the turn of the century.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 奥布莱恩住宅是世纪之交波士顿城市更新运动中少数未拆除的旧式木结构建筑之一

vocabulary.definition: adj. 微粒的；n. 微粒照片, 小粒迷幻药
vocabulary.context: But you can tell the plastic ones are artificial just from looking at pictures in the microdot library.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但只要看微点库中的图片就可以看出塑料是人造的

vocabulary.definition: n. 支级, 分段；[计] 子级, 次层
vocabulary.context: From there we took another elevator-bus down to main level, rode the monorail to Intercity Subway Station 27, and caught the second sublevel AA train to Boston.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从那里，我们乘坐另一辆电梯巴士下到主层，乘坐单轨列车到城际地铁站 27，然后搭乘第二趟地下 AA 列车前往波士顿

vocabulary.definition: n. 赚钱
vocabulary.context: When the news of the tree's discovery leaked out, quite a few sightseers stopped by to have a look at it, and the local government, realizing the money-making potential, began charging admission and advertising the place.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当发现这棵树的消息传出后，不少游客驻足观赏，当地政府也意识到了这棵树的赚钱潜力，开始收取入场费并进行广告宣传

vocabulary.definition: n. 分区制；[医] 带现象(补体结合)
vocabulary.context: When local officials arrived for an appraisal, they discovered that the house had a back yard, which is forbidden by zoning restrictions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当当地官员前来评估时，他们发现这所房子有一个后院，这是分区限制所禁止的

vocabulary.definition: n. 评价, 估价；[经] 评价, 评估
vocabulary.context: When local officials arrived for an appraisal, they discovered that the house had a back yard, which is forbidden by zoning restrictions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当当地官员前来评估时，他们发现这所房子有一个后院，这是分区限制所禁止的

vocabulary.definition: n. 刻度盘, 钟面, 转盘；v. 拨
vocabulary.context: Mom dialed a light breakfast of toast and synthetic milk so that we wouldn't waste time eating.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 妈妈给我们打了一份清淡的早餐，有吐司和合成奶，这样我们就不会浪费时间吃饭了

vocabulary.definition: n. 竖琴；v.i. 弹奏竖琴, 不停地说
vocabulary.context: Dad finally broke down and took us to East Boston Urban Center after Mom had been harping on it for the past two weeks.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在妈妈喋喋不休两周后，爸爸终于崩溃了，带我们去了东波士顿市中心

vocabulary.definition: n. stolid的变形
vocabulary.context: Comments on his stolidness were audible in every part of the room.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 房间里的每个角落都可以听到对他冷漠的评论

vocabulary.definition: adj. 简洁的, 简明的
vocabulary.context: Pittman started his testimony in a laconic and brusque manner.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 皮特曼以简洁而粗暴的方式开始了他的证词

vocabulary.definition: n. 救火队员, 消防队员；[机] 司炉
vocabulary.context: I saw the firemen coming up from the engine room.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我看到消防员从机舱出来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 唐突的, 直率的
vocabulary.context: Pittman started his testimony in a laconic and brusque manner.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 皮特曼以简洁而粗暴的方式开始了他的证词

vocabulary.definition: adv. 极好地, 美好地
vocabulary.context: All the passengers In my boat behaved admirably.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我船上的所有乘客都表现得令人钦佩

vocabulary.definition: v. 翻覆, 倾覆
vocabulary.context: Many of my passengers begged me not to do this, fearing that the boat would capsize.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的许多乘客恳求我不要这样做，担心船会倾覆

vocabulary.definition: n. 分隔间, 卧车包房, 防水舱隔；[医] 隔室, 隔间
vocabulary.context: I believed that only two or three of the compartments had filled and never haft the faintest idea that the Titanic would sink.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我相信只有两三个隔间已满，而且丝毫没有想到泰坦尼克号会沉没

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 给予, 传授, 告知
vocabulary.context: "If the Impart had been bow on," continued Pittman, "the Titanic would be afloat now.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果'Impart'号一直在船头，"皮特曼继续说道，"泰坦尼克号现在就会漂浮在水面上

vocabulary.definition: adv. 圆圆地, 直率地, 严厉地, 全面地
vocabulary.context: To do anything, one must have a special permit, and for every such permit he must pay roundly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 要做任何事，必须获得特别许可，并且必须为每一份此类许可全额付费

vocabulary.definition: n. 富矿带, 带来好运的事物, 幸运
vocabulary.context: Since the discovery of gold by George Carmack on Bonanza Creek in September 1896, the growth of this country has been phenomenal, more especially so to one who has visited and is familiar with Dawson and the Klondyke mining section.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自从 1896 年 9 月乔治·卡马克 (George Carmack) 在博南扎溪 (Bonanza Creek) 发现金矿以来，这个国家的发展是惊人的，对于曾经访问过并熟悉道森 (Dawson) 和克朗代克 (Klondyke) 矿区的人来说更是如此

vocabulary.definition: n. 地沟, 冲沟
vocabulary.context: On Skukum Gulch $30,000 were taken from two boxes of dirt.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 Skukum 峡谷，从两箱泥土中取出了 30,000 美元

vocabulary.definition: n. 地方, 所在, 位置；[法] 位置, 场所, 地区
vocabulary.context: In the meantime, men are sent to examine the locality and if anything of value is found, the man wishing to record the claim is told that it is already located.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与此同时，人们被派去检查该地点，如果发现任何有价值的东西，希望记录索赔的人会被告知该物品已经找到

vocabulary.definition: n. 隐匿, 隐蔽, 躲藏；[经] 隐瞒, 匿报
vocabulary.context: As to the entire yield of gold from the Klondyke Creeks, none can say except approximately; for the ten percent royalty imposed by the Canadian government has always met a phase of human nature which prompts to concealment and dishonesty, so that a truthful estimate cannot be made.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 至于克朗代克溪的黄金总产量，除了大约之外，没有人能说。因为加拿大政府征收的百分之十的特许权使用费总是会遇到人性的隐瞒和不诚实的阶段，从而无法做出真实的估计

vocabulary.definition: n. 平锅, 浅盘, 盆地, 硬土层, 拍摄全景；v. 上下左右移动, 摇镜头, 淘洗, 淘金
vocabulary.context: Charley Anderson, on Eldorado, panned out $700 in three hours.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 查理·安德森 (Charley Anderson) 在《埃尔多拉多》(Eldorado) 上三小时内赚到了 700 美元

vocabulary.definition: n. 不诚实；[法] 不诚实, 诈欺, 不正
vocabulary.context: As to the entire yield of gold from the Klondyke Creeks, none can say except approximately; for the ten percent royalty imposed by the Canadian government has always met a phase of human nature which prompts to concealment and dishonesty, so that a truthful estimate cannot be made.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 至于克朗代克溪的黄金总产量，除了大约之外，没有人能说。因为加拿大政府征收的百分之十的特许权使用费总是会遇到人性的隐瞒和不诚实的阶段，从而无法做出真实的估计

vocabulary.definition: adj. 现象的, 能知觉的, 异常的, 显著的, 惊人的
vocabulary.context: Since the discovery of gold by George Carmack on Bonanza Creek in September 1896, the growth of this country has been phenomenal, more especially so to one who has visited and is familiar with Dawson and the Klondyke mining section.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自从 1896 年 9 月乔治·卡马克 (George Carmack) 在博南扎溪 (Bonanza Creek) 发现金矿以来，这个国家的发展是惊人的，对于曾经访问过并熟悉道森 (Dawson) 和克朗代克 (Klondyke) 矿区的人来说更是如此

vocabulary.definition: n. 领土, 主权, 统治；[法] 统治权, 主权, 统治
vocabulary.context: The Canadian Dominion government is very oppressive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 加拿大自治领政府非常专制

vocabulary.definition: n. 学童
vocabulary.context: Chao said in her speech, "My hope is that their astounding achievement will become part of American folklore, known to every schoolchild and every American, in recognition of the vast and wonderful coat of many colors that makes our nation great."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 赵小兰在演讲中表示，"我希望他们的惊人成就将成为美国民间传说的一部分，为每个小学生和每个美国人所知，以表彰使我们国家变得伟大的巨大而美妙的多种颜色的外衣。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 路基
vocabulary.context: In this way, they blasted a roadbed for the railroad into the cliffs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就这样，他们把铁路的路基炸进了悬崖

vocabulary.definition: n. 痢疾；[医] 痢疾
vocabulary.context: They were able to avoid getting dysentery, a disease that came from drinking river water contaminated by human waste.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们能够避免感染痢疾，这是一种因饮用被人类排泄物污染的河水而产生的疾病

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 铲, 铲出（shovel的过去式与过去分词形式）
vocabulary.context: They also shoveled snow away from the rails and tunnels.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们还铲掉了铁轨和隧道上的积雪

vocabulary.definition: n. (建筑物的)砖石部分
vocabulary.context: He was skeptical about hiring Chinese workers, but Crocker told him, "Did they not build the Chinese Wall [the Great Wall of China], the biggest piece of masonry in the world?"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他对雇用中国工人持怀疑态度，但克罗克告诉他，"他们不是建造了世界上最大的砖石建筑--中国长城吗？"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 横贯大陆的, 大陆那边的
vocabulary.context: The best answer was to build a transcontinental railroad.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最好的答案是修建一条横贯大陆的铁路

vocabulary.definition: n. 领班, 工头, 陪审长；[经] 领班, 工头
vocabulary.context: James Harvey Strobridge was a railroad construction foreman at the time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 詹姆斯·哈维·斯特罗布里奇当时是一名铁路施工工头

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 弄污, 弄脏, 污染, 毒害；[化] 污染
vocabulary.context: They were able to avoid getting dysentery, a disease that came from drinking river water contaminated by human waste.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们能够避免感染痢疾，这是一种因饮用被人类排泄物污染的河水而产生的疾病

vocabulary.definition: n. 矫揉造作, 习性；[医] 装相, 作态
vocabulary.context: shy and quiet, especially in the presence of others conservative mannerisms, a higher-pitched voice, and a slight slouch.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 害羞、安静，尤其是在别人面前表现出保守的举止、声音较高、略显无精打采

vocabulary.definition: adj. 温和的, 温柔的
vocabulary.context: Clark was depicted as clumsy, shy, mild-mannered and frail.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 克拉克被描绘成笨拙、害羞、举止温和且脆弱

vocabulary.definition: n. 勋章, 徽章；[法] 勋章, 徽章, 荣誉
vocabulary.context: But within a short time, head artist Joe Shuster and his team began depicting Clark ripping open his shirt to reveal the "S" insignia on his chest - an image that became so popular that other superheroes would later copy the same type of transformations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但不久之后，首席艺术家乔·舒斯特（Joe Shuster）和他的团队开始描绘克拉克撕开衬衫，露出胸前的"S"徽章的形象--这一形象变得如此受欢迎，以至于其他超级英雄后来也效仿了同样类型的变形

vocabulary.definition: n. 个性内向者；v.t. 使内向, 使内弯, 内陷；v.i. 内省, 内弯；adj. 性格内向的
vocabulary.context: In order to make other people truly believe that he was a 2 3 "normal" civilian, Clark adopted a largely passive and introverted personality.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了让其他人真正相信他是一个 2 3 "正常"平民，克拉克采取了很大程度上被动和内向的性格

vocabulary.definition: n. 火车头, 机车；adj. 运转的, 火车头的, 移动的
vocabulary.context: Superman, by contrast, was described as "faster than a speeding bullet, more powerful than a locomotive, and able to leap tall buildings in a single bound" with significant powers including super-strength and x-ray vision.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相比之下，超人被描述为"比高速子弹更快，比机车更强大，能够一跃跳过高楼"，拥有超强力量和X射线视力等重要能力

vocabulary.definition: n. 没精打采的样子, 耷拉, 笨人；v.i. 没精打采地走, 耷拉着；v.t. 使低垂
vocabulary.context: shy and quiet, especially in the presence of others conservative mannerisms, a higher-pitched voice, and a slight slouch.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 害羞、安静，尤其是在别人面前表现出保守的举止、声音较高、略显无精打采

vocabulary.definition: adj. 消极的, 被动的, 冷漠的, 顺从的, 无源的；[医] 被动的
vocabulary.context: In order to make other people truly believe that he was a 2 3 "normal" civilian, Clark adopted a largely passive and introverted personality.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了让其他人真正相信他是一个 2 3 "正常"平民，克拉克采取了很大程度上被动和内向的性格

vocabulary.definition: n. 女朋友
vocabulary.context: Even Superman's girlfriend, Lois Lane, didn't know the full truth, although she sometimes suspected that Superman was actually Clark.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就连超人的女朋友露易丝·莱恩也不知道全部真相，尽管她有时怀疑超人实际上就是克拉克

vocabulary.definition: n. 被怀疑者, 嫌疑犯；adj. 令人怀疑的, 不可信的, 可疑的；v. 怀疑, 猜想
vocabulary.context: While the people in the city loved Superman, they assumed that he couldn't possibly have a secret identity because he didn't wear a mask, and thus very few people suspected that he and Clark could be the same person.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然这座城市的人们喜爱超人，但他们认为他不可能有秘密身份，因为他没有戴面具，因此很少有人怀疑他和克拉克可能是同一个人

vocabulary.definition: n. 黑色素；[化] 黑素
vocabulary.context: To counter this, Elliott lied to the children by stating that the melanin, responsible for making children blue-eyed, was also linked to their higher intelligence and learning ability.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了反驳这一点，埃利奥特对孩子们撒了谎，称导致孩子蓝眼睛的黑色素也与他们较高的智力和学习能力有关

vocabulary.definition: n. 帮助, 协助, 一份
vocabulary.context: She gave the blue-eyed children extra privileges, such as second helpings at lunch, access to the new jungle gym, and five extra minutes at recess.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她给了蓝眼睛的孩子们额外的特权，比如午餐时的第二份帮助、使用新的丛林健身房以及课间休息的额外五分钟

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 责骂, 惩罚；[法] 责罚, 惩戒, 严惩
vocabulary.context: Elliott would not allow brown-eyed and blue-eyed children to drink from the same water fountain, and often chastised the brown-eyed students when they did not follow the exercise's rules or made mistakes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 艾略特不允许棕色眼睛和蓝眼睛的孩子从同一个饮水机喝水，并且经常在棕色眼睛的学生不遵守练习规则或犯错误时惩罚他们

vocabulary.definition: adj. 蓝眼睛的, 心爱的
vocabulary.context: On that first day of the exercise, she designated the blue-eyed children as the superior group.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在演习的第一天，她指定蓝眼睛的孩子为优等组

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有帮助的, 有用的, 奉承的
vocabulary.context: The "inferior" classmates also transformed - into timid and subservient children who even during recess isolated themselves, including those who had previously been dominant in the class.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "劣等"同学也变成了胆怯、顺从的孩子，甚至在课间休息时也孤立自己，包括那些以前在班上占主导地位的孩子

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 例证, 例示；[法] 制作经公章证明的誊本, 例证
vocabulary.context: She often exemplified the differences between the two groups by 1.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她经常用1来举例说明两组之间的差异

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自大的, 傲慢的
vocabulary.context: Those who were deemed "superior" became arrogant, bossy, and otherwise unpleasant to their "inferior" classmates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那些被认为"优秀"的人变得傲慢、专横，并且在其他方面让他们的"低等"同学感到不愉快

vocabulary.definition: n. 辱骂, 嘲弄；v.t. 嘲弄, 奚落；adj. 很高的
vocabulary.context: While the brown-eyed children did taunt the blue-eyed in ways similar to what had occurred the previous day, Elliott reports it was much less intense.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然棕色眼睛的孩子确实以与前一天类似的方式嘲笑蓝眼睛的孩子，但埃利奥特报告说，程度要低得多

vocabulary.definition: adj. 使人不愉快的, 使人厌恶的, 煞风景的
vocabulary.context: Those who were deemed "superior" became arrogant, bossy, and otherwise unpleasant to their "inferior" classmates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那些被认为"优秀"的人变得傲慢、专横，并且在其他方面让他们的"低等"同学感到不愉快

vocabulary.definition: n. 小流氓；[法] 阿飞, 流氓, 街头恶棍
vocabulary.context: By the 1960s, an average of 25 hooligan incidents were being reported each year in England.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到了 20 世纪 60 年代，英格兰每年平均发生 25 起流氓事件

vocabulary.definition: adj. 过分热忱的
vocabulary.context: Football hooliganism refers to unruly, violent, and destructive behavior by overzealous supporters of soccer clubs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 足球流氓行为是指足球俱乐部狂热支持者的不守规矩、暴力和破坏性行为

vocabulary.definition: adj. 同心的, 同志的, 志趣相投的
vocabulary.context: Football hooliganism has factors in common with juvenile delinquency, and involvement is often the result of a person's need for group identity, interaction with like-minded people, feelings of legitimacy, and a desire for power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 足球流氓行为与青少年犯罪有共同的因素，而参与其中往往是一个人对群体认同的需要、与志同道合的人互动、合法感和权力欲望的结果

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非白种（人）的；n. 非白种人
vocabulary.context: In many countries, football hooliganism is also associated with racism, and abuse of non-white players is common.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在许多国家，足球流氓行为也与种族主义联系在一起，虐待非白人球员的现象很常见

vocabulary.definition: adv. 自发, 一时冲动, 出自自然, 不依赖人工, 自然产生, 本能, 自动, 自然而优雅
vocabulary.context: Participants often select locations away from the stadium to avoid arrest by the police, but conflict can also occur spontaneously inside the stadium or in the surrounding streets.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 参与者经常选择远离体育场的地点以避免被警察逮捕，但体育场内或周围街道也可能自发发生冲突

vocabulary.definition: n. 合法, 正统, 正当；[法] 合法性, 正统性, 婚生
vocabulary.context: Football hooliganism has factors in common with juvenile delinquency, and involvement is often the result of a person's need for group identity, interaction with like-minded people, feelings of legitimacy, and a desire for power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 足球流氓行为与青少年犯罪有共同的因素，而参与其中往往是一个人对群体认同的需要、与志同道合的人互动、合法感和权力欲望的结果

vocabulary.definition: n. 故意毁坏文物的行为, 破坏他人财产的行为
vocabulary.context: Behaviors include brawling, vandalism, and intimidation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 行为包括争吵、破坏公物和恐吓

vocabulary.definition: n. 发射物, 导弹, 飞弹, 火箭；adj. 可发射的
vocabulary.context: In December 2010, 14 people were injured when missiles were thrown onto the field and rocket flares were set off in the stands; two years later, a goalkeeper was attacked by a fan who had previously been banned from every soccer stadium in the UK.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2010年12月，向球场投掷导弹并在看台上引爆火箭弹，造成14人受伤；两年后，一名守门员遭到一名此前被禁止进入英国所有足球场的球迷的袭击

vocabulary.definition: n. 身份, 相同, 一致, 特性, 恒等式；[计] (打)标记, 标识
vocabulary.context: Football hooliganism has factors in common with juvenile delinquency, and involvement is often the result of a person's need for group identity, interaction with like-minded people, feelings of legitimacy, and a desire for power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 足球流氓行为与青少年犯罪有共同的因素，而参与其中往往是一个人对群体认同的需要、与志同道合的人互动、合法感和权力欲望的结果

vocabulary.definition: adj. 固执己见的, 武断的
vocabulary.context: But others condemned the piece: "By this example of opinionated, baseless reporting, Look itself pays scant recognition to the traditions of American Justice it claims were ignored," said one Mississippi reporter.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但其他人谴责这篇文章："通过这种固执己见、毫无根据的报道，《Look》本身对其声称被忽视的美国司法传统缺乏认可，"密西西比州的一位记者说

vocabulary.definition: n. 异父兄弟, 异母兄弟；[法] 同父异母或同母异父的兄弟
vocabulary.context: Bryant and his half-brother, J.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 布莱恩特和他同父异母的兄弟，J.

vocabulary.definition: adv. 动摇地；令人不安地
vocabulary.context: Even more disturbingly, white people have lynched thousands of other African Americans, many of whose names have been erased from history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更令人不安的是，白人对数千名非裔美国人处以私刑，其中许多人的名字已从历史中抹去

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无根据的
vocabulary.context: But others condemned the piece: "By this example of opinionated, baseless reporting, Look itself pays scant recognition to the traditions of American Justice it claims were ignored," said one Mississippi reporter.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但其他人谴责这篇文章："通过这种固执己见、毫无根据的报道，《Look》本身对其声称被忽视的美国司法传统缺乏认可，"密西西比州的一位记者说

vocabulary.definition: n. 诱拐, 绑架, 外展；[医] 外展, 展
vocabulary.context: Three days after the abduction, a fisherman discovered Emmett's body in the water of the Tallahatchie River.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 绑架三天后，一名渔民在塔拉哈奇河的水中发现了埃米特的尸体

vocabulary.definition: adj. 长达一年的, 一年之久的
vocabulary.context: Her action sparked the year-long Montgomery Bus Boycott, which brought the Civil Rights movement to the national stage.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的行为引发了长达一年的蒙哥马利巴士抵制运动，将民权运动推向了全国舞台

vocabulary.definition: adj. 天然的, 未成熟的, 粗糙的, 粗鲁的；n. 天然的物质
vocabulary.context: In 1955, Carolyn Bryant testified that Emmett grabbed her and made a crude comment to her.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1955年，卡罗琳·布莱恩特作证说，埃米特抓住了她，并对她做出了粗鲁的评论

vocabulary.definition: n. 凶手, 杀人者, 谋害者；[法] 谋杀犯, 凶手
vocabulary.context: Moses Wright testified against his nephew's murderers, becoming the first Black man to ever testify against white men in the state of Mississippi.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 摩西·赖特出庭指证谋杀他侄子的凶手，成为密西西比州第一个出庭指证白人的黑人

vocabulary.definition: n. 组织的破坏, 解体, 瓦解；[医] 结构破坏
vocabulary.context: He signed with the Kansas City Monarchs and had great success, but he was frustrated by all the disorganization that plagued the Negro Leagues.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他与堪萨斯城君主队签约并取得了巨大的成功，但他对困扰黑人联盟的所有组织混乱感到沮丧

vocabulary.definition: n. 不顺从, 反抗
vocabulary.context: He refused, and military police took him into custody for his insubordination.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他拒绝了，军警因他不服从命令而将他拘留

vocabulary.definition: n. 争吵, 试车, 插入部分, 接排；[化] 磨合运转; 磨合试车
vocabulary.context: He managed to escape a long jail sentence, but this and other run-ins with the police earned him a reputation as someone who was both eager and willing to stand up against racial oppression.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他设法逃脱了长期监禁，但这次以及其他与警方的冲突为他赢得了"渴望并愿意站起来反对种族压迫"的声誉

vocabulary.definition: n. 进入, 进入权, 准许进入；[化] 导纳
vocabulary.context: Other teams also disliked Robinson's admittance into the League.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他球队也不喜欢罗宾逊加入联盟

vocabulary.definition: n. 守垒员
vocabulary.context: As he stepped onto the field as first baseman in 1947, Jackie Robinson became the first Major League Baseball player to break the color barrier since 1880.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1947 年，杰基·罗宾逊 (Jackie Robinson) 作为一垒手踏上赛场，成为自 1880 年以来第一位打破肤色障碍的美国职业棒球大联盟 (MLB) 球员

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不利的, 敌对的, 相反的, 逆的；[法] 相反的, 敌对的, 逆的
vocabulary.context: In 1956 he did not dominate the league as much as he used to, partially because of the adverse effects of the diabetes he suffered from.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1956年，他不再像以前那样统治联盟，部分原因是他患有糖尿病的不利影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 监护, 拘留, 监禁；[经] 保管, 照顾, 保护
vocabulary.context: He refused, and military police took him into custody for his insubordination.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他拒绝了，军警因他不服从命令而将他拘留

vocabulary.definition: v. 支配, 占优势
vocabulary.context: In 1956 he did not dominate the league as much as he used to, partially because of the adverse effects of the diabetes he suffered from.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1956年，他不再像以前那样统治联盟，部分原因是他患有糖尿病的不利影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 病人, 残废者；adj. 有病的, 无效的
vocabulary.context: If the Justices decide the law does not line up with the Constitution, the law is invalid forever.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果大法官裁定该法律不符合宪法，该法律将永远无效

vocabulary.definition: n. 箴言, 座右铭, 警句
vocabulary.context: Its motto is, "Equal Justice under the Law," because it aims to interpret the Constitution and settle debates between lawyers fairly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其座右铭是"法律面前人人平等"，因为它的目的是解释宪法并公平解决律师之间的争论

vocabulary.definition: adj. 法庭的, 公正的, 审判上的, 司法的；[法] 司法的, 审判上的, 法官的
vocabulary.context: The Constitution set up the Supreme Court to be the highest court in the land, and the head of the judicial branch of the government.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 宪法规定最高法院为全国最高法院，也是政府司法部门的负责人

vocabulary.definition: n. 裁决, 判决, 判断性意见, 定论, 结论；[法] 定论, 判断, 意见
vocabulary.context: If one side of a case that was tried in a lower court does not like the outcome of the verdict, they can appeal to a higher court and hope the decision will be different.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果下级法院审理的案件的一方不喜欢判决结果，他们可以向上级法院提出上诉，希望得到不同的判决

vocabulary.definition: adj. 违反宪法的, 不符合宪法的；[法] 违反宪法的, 违宪的, 不合宪法规定的
vocabulary.context: They ruled that segregation in schools was unconstitutional.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们裁定学校的种族隔离违宪

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 解释, 演出, 翻译, 理解；v.i. 翻译, 解释
vocabulary.context: Its motto is, "Equal Justice under the Law," because it aims to interpret the Constitution and settle debates between lawyers fairly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其座右铭是"法律面前人人平等"，因为它的目的是解释宪法并公平解决律师之间的争论

vocabulary.definition: n. 字首, 首字母；adj. 开始的, 最初的, 字首的；v.t. 用姓名的首字母签名
vocabulary.context: The Justices meet immediately after the hearing to discuss some initial thoughts, but they do not officially vote on the case until sometime later.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 法官们在听证会后立即开会讨论一些初步想法，但直到稍后他们才会正式对此案进行投票

vocabulary.definition: adv. 作为公务员, 职务上, 官方地
vocabulary.context: The Justices meet immediately after the hearing to discuss some initial thoughts, but they do not officially vote on the case until sometime later.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 法官们在听证会后立即开会讨论一些初步想法，但直到稍后他们才会正式对此案进行投票

vocabulary.definition: n. 应用, 申请, 志愿书, 应用程序；[计] 应用, 应用程序
vocabulary.context: They get up to 8,000 applications every year from lawyers who want to appeal, but they only hear about 80 cases each year.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们每年收到多达8000份想要上诉的律师的申请，但他们每年只审理大约80个案件

vocabulary.definition: adj. 迁移的, 流浪的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 农民工的生活

vocabulary.definition: n. (尤指经济方面的)衰退, 下降趋势；[电] 低迷时期
vocabulary.context: The revolution also caused an economic downturn in Mexico, and many workers realized they could earn more money in the United States than they could in their home country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这场革命还导致了墨西哥的经济衰退，许多工人意识到他们在美国可以比在自己的祖国赚到更多的钱

vocabulary.definition: n. 签证；v.t. 签发

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 政府制定了一项向移民提供短期工作签证的计划

vocabulary.definition: n. 杀虫剂；[化] 农药

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大部分农场没有卫生间设施，没有水供工人饮用，也没有地方可以清洗手上的危险农药

vocabulary.definition: adv. 技术上, 学术上, 专门地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然这在技术上是非法的，但一些孩子不得不与父母一起在田里长时间工作

vocabulary.definition: adj. 短期的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 政府制定了一项向移民提供短期工作签证的计划

vocabulary.definition: n. 国籍, 市民权, 市民的身份；[法] 公民权, 公民资格, 公民身分
vocabulary.context: Most workers relied on these visas to stay in the country, but some stayed long enough to earn their citizenship and have children.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大多数工人依靠这些签证留在该国，但有些人停留了足够长的时间以获得公民身份并生孩子

vocabulary.definition: n. 温文, 优雅；优美

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的舞蹈是如此美丽，以至于它的名声传遍了全国，各地的精英女性都想效仿她的优雅

vocabulary.definition: n. 畸形足；[医] 畸形足
vocabulary.context: One story relates that during the Shang dynasty, the concubine Daji, who was said to have clubfoot, asked the Emperor to make foot binding mandatory for all girls in court so that her own feet would be the standard of beauty and elegance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有一个故事说，商朝时期，据说患有畸形足的妲己妃子请求皇帝强制所有宫女缠足，让自己的脚成为美丽和优雅的标准

vocabulary.definition: adj. 高跟的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是如果你被要求把脚塞进只有 4 英寸长的高跟尖头鞋里怎么办？

vocabulary.definition: adj. (鞋类或衣服)有襻的,有带子的
vocabulary.context: Stiletto pumps, strappy sandals, and 6-inch heels have graced fashion runways for decades.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几十年来，细高跟鞋、系带凉鞋和 6 英寸高跟鞋一直为时尚秀场增光添彩

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 缩小, 萎缩; [医]向内生长的
vocabulary.context: Despite careful maintenance of the toenails, sometimes a toenail could become ingrown and lead to a serious infection, which, if improperly treated, could spread through the blood or bones and in some cases become lethal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管仔细保养脚趾甲，有时脚趾甲可能会向内生长并导致严重感染，如果治疗不当，可能会通过血液或骨骼传播，在某些情况下会致命

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未卷绕的, 未上发条的, 松散的；unwind的过去式和过去分词
vocabulary.context: The bandages had to be unwound at least every other day to clean the foot and check for infection, then re-wound even more tightly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 绷带必须至少每隔一天解开一次，以清洁足部并检查是否感染，然后重新缠绕得更紧

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 效法, 尽力赶上, 同...竞争；[计] 仿真
vocabulary.context: Her dance was so beautiful that word of it spread throughout the country, and elite women everywhere wanted to emulate her gracefulness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她的舞蹈是如此美丽，以至于它的名声传遍了全国，各地的精英女性都想效仿她的优雅

vocabulary.definition: n. 特技, 绝技, 花招, 噱头, 手腕, 发育不良；v.t. 阻碍成长；v.i. 表演特技
vocabulary.context: Foot binding is the custom of wrapping a young girl's feet tightly and painfully in strips of cloth to stunt future growth of the foot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 缠足是一种用布条将年轻女孩的脚紧紧包裹起来的习俗，以阻碍脚的未来生长

vocabulary.definition: adv. 痛苦地, 苦恼地, 费力地
vocabulary.context: Foot binding is the custom of wrapping a young girl's feet tightly and painfully in strips of cloth to stunt future growth of the foot.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 缠足是一种用布条将年轻女孩的脚紧紧包裹起来的习俗，以阻碍脚的未来生长

vocabulary.definition: n. 亮晶晶的小物件；v. (使)闪烁
vocabulary.context: And floats aloft our spangled flag, in glory and in pride,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在荣耀和骄傲中飘扬着我们闪亮的旗帜，

vocabulary.context: Long moving lines of rising dust your vision may descry;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你的视线可能会看到长长的移动的扬尘线；

vocabulary.definition: adv. 踏实地, 不变地; 岿然; 坚定不渝
vocabulary.context: We dare not look behind us, but steadfastly before:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们不敢回头看，而是坚定地向前看：

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 刺刀在阳光下闪闪发光，乐队奏出勇敢的音乐：

vocabulary.definition: n. 发声, 言辞, 表达, 说话的方式, 死；[医] 发言, 言语, 语调
vocabulary.context: With hearts too full for utterance, with but a silent tear;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 心事重重，无言以泪；

vocabulary.definition: adv. 在高处, 在上
vocabulary.context: And floats aloft our spangled flag, in glory and in pride,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在荣耀和骄傲中飘扬着我们闪亮的旗帜，

vocabulary.definition: n. 扳钳, 扳手, 扭伤, 歪曲, 痛苦；v.t. 猛扭, 扭伤, 曲解, 折磨；v.i. 猛扭, 猛绞
vocabulary.context: Or from foul treason's savage group to wrench the murderous blade,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 或是从奸诈叛逆的野蛮集团手中扳动杀气之刃，
