vocabulary.contextTranslation: 面对外国敌人，它的碎片值得游行；

vocabulary.definition: n. 北方人；adj. 北方的, 向北的, 自北方来的
vocabulary.context: If you look across the hill tops that meet the Northern sky,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果你眺望与北方天空交汇的山顶，

vocabulary.definition: n. 用法, 使用, 习惯, 惯用法, 习惯法；[经] 使用情况, 使用用途, 惯例
vocabulary.context: A credit score keeps track of how often you are not able to pay back your full credit card bill right away, as well as other data related to your credit usage.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 信用评分记录您无法立即偿还全部信用卡账单的频率，以及与您的信用使用相关的其他数据

vocabulary.definition: n. 月刊；adj. 每月的, 每月一次的
vocabulary.context: It is usually not smart to pay off only small portions of your monthly credit card bill.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只还清每月信用卡账单的一小部分通常是不明智的

vocabulary.definition: n. 赌博, 冒险；v. 赌博, 孤注一掷
vocabulary.context: You are taking a gamble - if Facebook does well in the next year, the value of those stocks will go up, but if it does poorly, their value will go down.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你是在赌博--如果 Facebook 明年表现出色，这些股票的价值就会上涨，但如果表现不佳，它们的价值就会下跌

vocabulary.definition: n. 存款, 挽救, 节约；adj. 搭救的, 节约的, 保留的, 补偿的；prep. 除...之外
vocabulary.context: The second option is to use a savings account.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第二种选择是使用储蓄账户

vocabulary.definition: n. 百分比, 比率, 部分, 可能性；[计] 百分比
vocabulary.context: The stock market is a place where you can buy stocks, or very small percentages, of big companies like Walmart or Facebook.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 股票市场是您可以购买沃尔玛或 Facebook 等大公司股票或极小比例股票的地方

vocabulary.definition: adj. 最小的, 最低的；n. 最小值；[计] 最小值
vocabulary.context: So, in the end, you might owe $4,500 (plus the $1,000 you used to pay the minimum part of the original bill).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，最终您可能欠下 4,500 美元（加上您用于支付原始账单最低部分的 1,000 美元）

vocabulary.definition: adv. 贫穷地, 不充分地, 贫乏地；adj. 身体不舒服的
vocabulary.context: You are taking a gamble - if Facebook does well in the next year, the value of those stocks will go up, but if it does poorly, their value will go down.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你是在赌博--如果 Facebook 明年表现出色，这些股票的价值就会上涨，但如果表现不佳，它们的价值就会下跌

vocabulary.definition: adj. 争论的, 论争的, 被议论的
vocabulary.context: Money is one of the most complicated and controversial things in the world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金钱是世界上最复杂、最有争议的事物之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 报告, 解释, 估价, 理由, 利润, 算账, 帐目；v.i. 报帐, 解释, 导致, 报偿, 占, 杀死；v.t. 认为；[计] 帐户, 帐号
vocabulary.context: The first option is to put money into a checking account.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第一个选择是将钱存入支票账户

vocabulary.definition: n. 倾注, 输血, 输液；[医] 输血, 输液
vocabulary.context: John Beattie, began to examine the problems regarding blood transfusions and the storage of blood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 约翰·贝蒂（John Beattie）开始研究有关输血和血液储存的问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 遗腹的, 死后的；[医] 遗腹的
vocabulary.context: He has received numerous posthumous awards and was featured in the United States Postal Service's Great American Stamp Series in 1981.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他死后获得了无数奖项，并于 1981 年出现在美国邮政局的伟大美国邮票系列中

vocabulary.definition: n. 血浆, 淋巴液, 原生质, 等离子体；[化] 等离体; 等离子体
vocabulary.context: While blood itself was difficult to preserve, Drew discovered that blood plasma - the blood without its cells - could last for much longer than whole blood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然血液本身很难保存，但德鲁发现血浆（不含细胞的血液）可以比全血保存更长时间

vocabulary.definition: n. 诊所, 临床教学；[医] 诊所(门诊部); 临床(讲解); 临床(学)科
vocabulary.context: Sadly, on April 1, 1950, while Drew and three other physicians were travelling to an annual free clinic in Tuskegee, Alabama, the car that Drew was driving crashed near Burlington, North Carolina.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 可悲的是，1950 年 4 月 1 日，当德鲁和其他三名医生前往阿拉巴马州塔斯基吉的年度免费诊所时，德鲁驾驶的汽车在北卡罗来纳州伯灵顿附近坠毁

vocabulary.definition: n. 博士头衔
vocabulary.context: Drew left Columbia University having made significant contributions to medical science and having become the first African American to earn a doctorate degree in medicine from the school.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 德鲁离开哥伦比亚大学时对医学做出了重大贡献，并成为第一位从该校获得医学博士学位的非裔美国人

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 制止, 吓住, 威慑
vocabulary.context: Drew would not let any of this deter him.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 德鲁不会让这一切阻止他

vocabulary.definition: n. 教员, 讲师；[法] 指导者, 教员, 讲师
vocabulary.context: He worked as a biology instructor and coach at Morgan College in Baltimore, Maryland for two years before applying to medical school and enrolling at McGill University in Montreal, Canada.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他在马里兰州巴尔的摩的摩根学院担任了两年的生物学讲师和教练，然后申请进入医学院并就读于加拿大蒙特利尔的麦吉尔大学

vocabulary.definition: n. 条, 棒, 酒吧, 栅, 障碍物；v.t. 禁止, 阻挡, 妨碍；[计] 棒形图
vocabulary.context: At that time, many of the medical schools in the United States barred African American students from entering their programs and Drew could not afford the high costs that came with attending classes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当时，美国许多医学院禁止非裔美国学生进入其课程，德鲁无法承担上课所带来的高昂费用

vocabulary.definition: n. 伤害, 帮倒忙的行为
vocabulary.context: Not only did it do a disservice to those who did come to Kitty's aid, it also gave America a false impression of uncaring city life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这不仅对那些前来援助基蒂的人造成了伤害，而且还给美国留下了城市生活冷漠的错误印象

vocabulary.definition: n. 有柄大杯, 脸, 苦读者；v.t. 给...拍照, 攻击, 苦读；v.i. 拼命用功
vocabulary.context: Psychologists John Darley and Bibb Latané heard Genovese's story and used it as inspiration for a series of experiments on what they named the "bystander effect." In the experiments, they placed a test subject in a large crowd and staged an emergency situation (like a mugging or a someone fainting).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 心理学家约翰·达利（John Darley）和比伯·拉塔内（Bibb Latané）听到了吉诺维斯的故事，并将其作为一系列实验的灵感，他们称之为"旁观者效应"。在实验中，他们将测试对象放在一大群人中，并上演紧急情况（例如抢劫或有人晕倒）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 心不在焉的, 不注意的
vocabulary.context: Not only did it do a disservice to those who did come to Kitty's aid, it also gave America a false impression of uncaring city life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这不仅对那些前来援助基蒂的人造成了伤害，而且还给美国留下了城市生活冷漠的错误印象

vocabulary.definition: n. 布局, 陈列, 规划图, 页面布局, 版面编排；[计] 页面布局, 版面
vocabulary.context: Because of the layout of the apartment complex, the crime was difficult for any neighbors to see.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于公寓大楼的布局，犯罪行为很难被任何邻居看到

vocabulary.definition: n. 不活动, 无为, 怠惰, 迟钝；[医] 无作用
vocabulary.context: Some scientists also argue that the existence of "the bystander effect" allows people to justify their own inaction in times of crisis.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些科学家还认为，"旁观者效应"的存在使人们可以为自己在危机时刻的不作为辩护

vocabulary.definition: n. 满足, 自鸣得意, 自满
vocabulary.context: The story garnered national attention; readers were horrified at the utter complacency of these bystanders.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个故事引起了全国的关注；读者们对这些旁观者的完全自满感到震惊

vocabulary.definition: n. 夜盗行为, 盗窃, 盗窃行为；[法] 夜盗行为, 夜间入屋窃盗罪, 窃盗
vocabulary.context: He also admitted to several previous rapes and "30 or 40 burglaries." A jury sentenced him to serve life in prison.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他还承认了之前的几起强奸案和"30或40起入室盗窃案"。陪审团判处他终身监禁

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 理解, 忧虑, 逮捕；v.i. 担心, 理解
vocabulary.context: Moseley was apprehended by the police six days after the attack, which he admitted to, saying he had been looking for an easy victim who wouldn't fight back.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 袭击发生六天后，莫斯利被警方逮捕，他承认这一点，并称他一直在寻找一个不会反击的容易受害人

vocabulary.definition: v.i. vt. 对...作双重检验, 双重把关；[经] 复核
vocabulary.context: They also employ many different editors to double-check all of the facts cited in the articles.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们还聘请了许多不同的编辑来仔细检查文章中引用的所有事实

vocabulary.definition: n. 博客, 网志（网站上记有活动、意见等的个人记录）( blog的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: The news media includes newspapers, magazines, television news shows, radio news shows, and Internet sources such as online newspapers and independent blogs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 新闻媒体包括报纸、杂志、电视新闻节目、广播新闻节目以及网络报纸和独立博客等互联网来源

vocabulary.definition: n. 偏见, 斜纹；adj. 偏斜的；adv. 偏斜；v.t. 使有偏见；[计] 偏流; 偏压; 偏磁; 偏离
vocabulary.context: They are worried that news stories might be biased.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们担心新闻报道可能存在偏见

vocabulary.definition: adv. 根本地, 完全地
vocabulary.context: The Internet has radically changed how news sources communicate with their audience, and it has made it harder to define "news media" exactly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 互联网从根本上改变了新闻来源与受众的沟通方式，并且使得准确定义"新闻媒体"变得更加困难

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可靠的；[法] 不可靠的, 靠不住的
vocabulary.context: Reporters gather facts and write about them - so why do some people say the news is unreliable?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 记者收集事实并报道它们--为什么有些人说新闻不可靠？

vocabulary.definition: n. [通信]网站( website的复数形式 )
vocabulary.context: They can read news from multiple sources, or they can look up facts on their own to make sure the news matches other websites and records.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们可以从多个来源阅读新闻，也可以自己查找事实以确保新闻与其他网站和记录匹配

vocabulary.definition: n. 出口, 发泄方法, 市场；[医] 出口
vocabulary.context: There are also regional news outlets; for example, the Los Angeles Times is based in Los Angeles, California, but reports on issues relevant to the whole state and sometimes the whole country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有地区新闻媒体；例如，《洛杉矶时报》总部位于加利福尼亚州洛杉矶，但报道与整个州甚至整个国家相关的问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有关联的, 有关系的, 适当的, 相应的；[法] 有关的, 相关的
vocabulary.context: There are also regional news outlets; for example, the Los Angeles Times is based in Los Angeles, California, but reports on issues relevant to the whole state and sometimes the whole country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有地区新闻媒体；例如，《洛杉矶时报》总部位于加利福尼亚州洛杉矶，但报道与整个州甚至整个国家相关的问题

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 通知, 使了解, 使充满；v.i. 提供资料, 告发
vocabulary.context: They also keep the public informed about what the government is doing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们还让公众了解政府正在做什么

vocabulary.definition: n. 农业工人, 雇农
vocabulary.context: Many Mexican Americans worked as migrant farmworkers who traveled from farm to farm harvesting different crops according to the season.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 许多墨西哥裔美国人都是农民工，他们从一个农场到另一个农场，根据季节收获不同的农作物

vocabulary.definition: adv. 第三
vocabulary.context: Thirdly, schools in poorer areas, which sometimes had a higher percentage of Chicano students, received less funding than schools in rich, whiter areas.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第三，较贫困地区的学校（有时奇卡诺学生比例较高）获得的资金少于富裕白人地区的学校

vocabulary.definition: n. 医疗保健；卫生保健；健康护理, 健康服务
vocabulary.context: For example, in the early 1970s there was a whole group of women dedicated to making sure healthcare forms were available in Spanish as well as in English.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，在 20 世纪 70 年代初，有一群女性致力于确保提供西班牙语和英语的医疗保健表格

vocabulary.definition: adj. 西南的, 向西南的, 来自西南的, 西南部的
vocabulary.context: The movement mainly took place in Southwestern states - such as Texas, New Mexico, and California - and the roots of the struggle ran deep.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这场运动主要发生在德克萨斯州、新墨西哥州和加利福尼亚州等西南部各州，而且斗争的根源很深

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使成通俗性, 使平易, 使普及；v.i. 通俗化
vocabulary.context: They popularized the notion of Aztlán, an area in northern Mexico and southern United States, thought to be the origin land of the Mexican people.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们普及了阿兹特兰的概念，阿兹特兰是墨西哥北部和美国南部的一个地区，被认为是墨西哥人民的起源地

vocabulary.definition: adj. 祖先的, 祖传的；[法] 祖先的, 祖传的
vocabulary.context: However, they were unsuccessful, so they began promoting the idea of sacred, ancestral lands.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，他们没有成功，因此他们开始宣扬神圣的祖传土地的想法

vocabulary.definition: n. 管理人, 行政官；[经] 遗产管理人员
vocabulary.context: They walked out of classes on school campuses and negotiated with school administrators and political leaders for education reform.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们走出校园的课堂，与学校管理人员和政治领导人就教育改革进行谈判

vocabulary.definition: n. 娱乐, 款待, 娱乐表演
vocabulary.context: The movement accomplished many of its goals over several decades, and it is still working today to promote fairer treatment of Mexican Americans in schools, jobs, and even in entertainment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该运动在几十年来实现了许多目标，至今仍在努力促进墨西哥裔美国人在学校、工作甚至娱乐领域受到更公平的待遇

vocabulary.definition: n. 麻疯病, 腐败；[医] 麻风
vocabulary.context: She opened hospice locations, so even the poor of the city could "die like angels - loved and wanted." She gave medical care to people with leprosy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她开设了临终关怀中心，这样即使是城里的穷人也能"像天使一样死去--被爱着、被需要"。她为麻风病患者提供医疗服务

vocabulary.definition: n. 旅客住宿处, 收容所
vocabulary.context: She opened hospice locations, so even the poor of the city could "die like angels - loved and wanted." She gave medical care to people with leprosy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她开设了临终关怀中心，这样即使是城里的穷人也能"像天使一样死去--被爱着、被需要"。她为麻风病患者提供医疗服务

vocabulary.definition: n. 女修道会；[法] 召集, 召开, 开会
vocabulary.context: Teresa's school, which was near her convent.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特蕾莎修女的学校，靠近她的修道院

vocabulary.definition: n. 孤儿院, 孤儿身份；[法] 孤儿身份, 孤儿, 孤儿院
vocabulary.context: She also opened orphanages for the many homeless children who lived on the streets.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她还为许多流落街头的无家可归的儿童开设了孤儿院

vocabulary.definition: adj. 注意的, 留心的
vocabulary.context: Others questioned her hospitals' practices, stating that the nuns were not always attentive and often used unhygienic equipment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他人质疑她所在医院的做法，称修女们并不总是专心，而且经常使用不卫生的设备

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 赞美, 美化, 赞扬, 使辉煌
vocabulary.context: Some activists in India criticized Teresa for glorifying poverty and suffering instead of trying to end it once and for all.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 印度的一些活动人士批评特蕾莎修女美化贫困和苦难，而不是试图一劳永逸地结束它

vocabulary.definition: n. 部, 内阁, 服务；[经] 部
vocabulary.context: Although her ministry proved to be extremely difficult in the first few years, the Catholic church granted her some financial support, and her charity became more famous.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管最初几年她的事工极其困难，但天主教会给予了她一些经济支持，她的慈善事业也变得更加出名

vocabulary.definition: adv. 亲自地, 个别地, 当面, 就本人而言, 针对个人地
vocabulary.context: Later, she personally traveled to Eastern Europe in order to start Missionaries of Charity branches in several unwelcoming Communist countries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 后来，她亲自前往东欧，在几个不受欢迎的共产主义国家建立仁爱传教修会分会

vocabulary.definition: adj. 反主流文化的, 反正统文化的
vocabulary.context: This love for the strange and unusual bled into his artwork; he experimented with many styles of art, including Cubism and Dada, a countercultural art movement that said wars were caused by peoples' reliance on logic, reason, and money.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种对陌生和不寻常的事物的热爱渗透到了他的艺术作品中。他尝试了多种艺术风格，包括立体主义和达达主义，这是一种反文化艺术运动，认为战争是由人们对逻辑、理性和金钱的依赖引起的

vocabulary.definition: n. 超现实主义；[法] 超现实主义
vocabulary.context: When Dalí met painter Joan Miró in 1929, he was introduced to the style of painting he would use for the rest of his career: Surrealism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1929年，当达利遇到画家胡安·米罗时，他了解到了他将在余下的职业生涯中使用的绘画风格：超现实主义

vocabulary.definition: n. 潜水服；防寒泳衣；湿式保暖服
vocabulary.context: He attended events in outlandish costumes -he once gave an important lecture while wearing a wetsuit and walking two Russian wolfhounds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他穿着奇装异服参加活动--有一次，他穿着潜水衣，遛着两只俄罗斯狼犬，发表了一次重要的演讲

vocabulary.definition: n. 猎狼狗
vocabulary.context: He attended events in outlandish costumes -he once gave an important lecture while wearing a wetsuit and walking two Russian wolfhounds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他穿着奇装异服参加活动--有一次，他穿着潜水衣，遛着两只俄罗斯狼犬，发表了一次重要的演讲

vocabulary.definition: adj. 被牢记的
vocabulary.context: His incredible talent and his unique, eccentric personality are some of the most well-remembered details in art history.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他令人难以置信的才华和他独特、古怪的个性是艺术史上最被人们铭记的细节

vocabulary.definition: n. 细查, 讨论, 游说；v.t. 彻底检查, 向...拉票或拉生意, 讨论；v.i. 游说
vocabulary.context: After he moved back to Europe in 1948 he spent the next 15 years working on 19 large canvasses that combined classical attention to detail with surrealist images and subject matter.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1948 年回到欧洲后，他在接下来的 15 年里创作了 19 幅大型画布，将古典主义对细节的关注与超现实主义图像和主题结合起来

vocabulary.definition: n. 连鬓胡子, 鬓脚
vocabulary.context: He grew sideburns and wore dramatic coats and stockings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他蓄起了鬓角，穿着引人注目的外套和袜子

vocabulary.definition: n. 古怪, 古怪行为, 怪癖；[化] 偏心度; 偏心距; 偏心率
vocabulary.context: At an early age, he expressed some of the eccentricities and emotional outbursts that would later make him famous.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在很小的时候，他就表现出了一些怪癖和情绪爆发，这些让他后来成名

vocabulary.definition: adv. 令人难忘地；感人地
vocabulary.context: Famous for his impressively long mustache, Dalí impressed the world with his creativity, unique style, and thought-provoking imagery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 达利以其令人印象深刻的长胡子而闻名，他的创造力、独特的风格和发人深省的意象给世界留下了深刻的印象

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无意义的, 荒谬的
vocabulary.context: His paintings used unexpected, sometimes nonsensical images to show people that there was more to the human mind than simple realism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的画作使用了意想不到的、有时甚至是荒谬的图像来向人们展示人类的思想不仅仅是简单的现实主义

vocabulary.definition: num. 数百万
vocabulary.context: Athletes didn't come home to multi-million dollar endorsements deals then as they do now.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那时的运动员并没有像现在那样接受数百万美元的代言交易

vocabulary.definition: adj. 停息的, 盛开过的, 浮夸的；overblow的过去分词
vocabulary.context: Instead of stories about German successes, the papers were filled with articles about Owens breaking records and Hitler's overblown response to his wins.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 报纸上没有关于德国成功的故事，而是充斥着关于欧文斯打破记录以及希特勒对其胜利的过分反应的文章

vocabulary.definition: n. 小佃农
vocabulary.context: But what Jesse Owens - the son of a sharecropper and the grandson of slaves - did was particularly special because of when and where he did it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是杰西·欧文斯--佃农的儿子和奴隶的孙子--所做的事情特别特别，因为他做的时间和地点

vocabulary.definition: n. 支持, 认可, 背书；[经] 背书, 担保, 保证
vocabulary.context: Athletes didn't come home to multi-million dollar endorsements deals then as they do now.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那时的运动员并没有像现在那样接受数百万美元的代言交易

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无比的, 无匹敌的, 不相配的；[化] 不匹配的
vocabulary.context: His feats remained unmatched until the 1984 Summer Olympics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 直到 1984 年夏季奥运会，他的壮举一直无人能及

vocabulary.definition: n. 小尖塔, 尖峰, 高峰, 极点；v.t. 造小尖塔, 置于尖顶上
vocabulary.context: Owens enjoyed running because, as he said, "it was something you could do by yourself, and under your own power." It was this mindset that would lead him to the pinnacle of athletics, where he would face even more challenges.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 欧文斯喜欢跑步，因为正如他所说，"这是你可以通过自己的力量完成的事情。"正是这种心态将他带向了田径运动的顶峰，在那里他将面临更多的挑战

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 迫近, 威胁, 悬挂
vocabulary.context: But the games and the excitement surrounding them continued in spite of the impending war.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管战争迫在眉睫，但比赛和围绕比赛的兴奋仍在继续

vocabulary.definition: n. 运动员, 运动选手；[医] 运动员
vocabulary.context: Hitler criticized the United States for including athletes of color and Jewish athletes on the roster.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 希特勒批评美国将有色人种运动员和犹太运动员列入名单

vocabulary.definition: n. 克里奥耳人, 克里奥耳语, 克里奥耳人所讲的法语；adj. 克里奥耳人的, 克里奥耳式法语的
vocabulary.context: To protest the law, a group of concerned black, Creole, and white Louisiana citizens, called the Committee of Citizens, convinced Plessy to intentionally buy a ticket for a whites-only car.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了抗议这项法律，一群关心此事的路易斯安那州黑人、克里奥尔人和白人公民组成了公民委员会，说服普莱西故意购买白人专用汽车的票

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不一致的, 易变的, 前后矛盾的；[法] 矛盾的, 不一致的, 不协调的
vocabulary.context: The law, he argued, was "inconsistent with the personal liberty of citizens, white and black, in that state, and hostile to both the spirit and letter of the constitution of the United States." He predicted that the Plessy decision would...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他认为，该法律"与该州白人和黑人公民的个人自由不一致，并且违背美国宪法的精神和文字。"他预测普莱西的决定将......

vocabulary.definition: n. 本垒打, 传信鸽
vocabulary.context: The case began in 1892 when a man named Homer Plessy purchased a first-class train ticket for a whites-only car in Louisiana.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此案始于 1892 年，当时一位名叫荷马·普莱西 (Homer Plessy) 的男子在路易斯安那州购买了一辆白人专用车厢的头等火车票

vocabulary.definition: adv. 明确地；明白地
vocabulary.context: Laws that explicitly segregated the races could not be challenged in court anymore; the Supreme Court had given segregation the legal "okay," and states took advantage of this to establish segregation for decades to come.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 明确种族隔离的法律不能再在法庭上受到质疑；最高法院在法律上给予种族隔离"可以"，各州利用这一点在未来几十年内建立了种族隔离

vocabulary.definition: n. 先例, 前例；adj. 在先的, 在前的
vocabulary.context: ...He predicted that the Plessy decision would become one of the most infamous cases in Supreme Court history, and that it would set a precedent of segregation across the country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...他预测普莱西案的判决将成为最高法院历史上最臭名昭著的案件之一，并将开创全国种族隔离的先例

vocabulary.definition: n. 命令, 指令, 要求；v.t. 委任统治
vocabulary.context: His lawyers argued that the law mandating rail car segregation was unconstitutional because of the 14th Amendment, which ensured equal protection under the law for all citizens.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的律师辩称，由于第十四修正案确保所有公民受到法律的平等保护，因此强制实施铁路车厢隔离的法律是违宪的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 普遍的, 流行的
vocabulary.context: Although segregation occurred in northern states, especially in public school systems, it was most prevalent in the South.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管种族隔离发生在北部各州，特别是在公立学校系统中，但在南部最为普遍

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 优待；照顾
vocabulary.context: He believed it was wrong to undermine the 14th Amendment in this way, when the majority of the country had favored the new law.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他认为，当全国大多数人都支持新法律时，以这种方式破坏第十四修正案是错误的

vocabulary.definition: n. 茄属植物；[医] 茄属植物
vocabulary.context: So, all things nightshade: eggplants and peppers and tomatoes and tomatillos."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，所有茄属植物：茄子、辣椒、西红柿和粘浆果。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 强壮, 胆子, 勇气, 耐寒(性), 耐劳(性)
vocabulary.context: With help from the sun, the inside of the tunnel becomes a region with what's called a good hardiness zone - a standard the USDA uses to describe places where certain plants grow best.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在阳光的帮助下，隧道内部变成了一个具有良好耐寒区的区域--美国农业部用来描述某些植物生长最佳的地方的标准

vocabulary.definition: adj. 铺满整个地板的, 无处不在的
vocabulary.context: She guides me through rows of wall-to-wall and floor-to-ceiling greenery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她引导我穿过一排排从墙到墙、从地板到天花板的绿色植物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 最北的
vocabulary.context: Community greenhouses have even popped up above the Arctic Circle in Alaska's northernmost village, Barrow.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 社区温室甚至在阿拉斯加最北端的村庄巴罗的北极圈上空出现

vocabulary.definition: n. 茄子
vocabulary.context: So, all things nightshade: eggplants and peppers and tomatoes and tomatillos."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，所有茄属植物：茄子、辣椒、西红柿和粘浆果。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 绿叶, 绿树, 装饰用的绿树枝或叶
vocabulary.context: She guides me through rows of wall-to-wall and floor-to-ceiling greenery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她引导我穿过一排排从墙到墙、从地板到天花板的绿色植物

vocabulary.definition: n. 胡椒粉, 胡椒, 辣椒；[化] 胡椒; 辣椒; 花椒
vocabulary.context: So, all things nightshade: eggplants and peppers and tomatoes and tomatillos."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，所有茄属植物：茄子、辣椒、西红柿和粘浆果。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 农业；[机] 农业, 农学
vocabulary.context: In fact, agriculture is booming in the 49th state.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，第 49 个州的农业正在蓬勃发展

vocabulary.definition: n. 基金, 资金, 存款, 财源, 贮藏；v.t. 提供资金, 积累
vocabulary.context: Because in the last seven years, nearly 700 giant greenhouses have popped up there, thanks to a program funded by the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因为在过去七年里，由于美国资助的一项计划，那里出现了近 700 个巨型温室

vocabulary.definition: adj. 硫的
vocabulary.context: During your visit, you would spot hundreds of extinct volcanoes, along with omnipresent yellow clouds full of sulphuric acid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在您访问期间，您会看到数百座死火山，以及无处不在的充满硫酸的黄色云

vocabulary.definition: n. 太空衣
vocabulary.context: Once you've donned your spacesuit, don't expect a glass of water.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一旦你穿上宇航服，就不要期待一杯水

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无所不在的, 普通存在的
vocabulary.context: During your visit, you would spot hundreds of extinct volcanoes, along with omnipresent yellow clouds full of sulphuric acid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在您访问期间，您会看到数百座死火山，以及无处不在的充满硫酸的黄色云

vocabulary.definition: n. 世人, 俗人, 俗物
vocabulary.context: Venus may be our next door neighbor, but a visit to Venus won't be very pleasant for us Earthlings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金星可能是我们的隔壁邻居，但对我们地球人来说，访问金星并不是一件很愉快的事

vocabulary.definition: adj. 乳汁的, 银河的, 极大的；[医] 乳液的, 催乳液的
vocabulary.context: Well, Earth also has neighbors - in fact, Earth has millions of stars, planets, moons, asteroids, and other planets as fellow residents in our galactic neighborhood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 好吧，地球也有邻居--事实上，地球有数百万颗恒星、行星、卫星、小行星和其他行星，它们是我们银河系邻居的居民

vocabulary.definition: n. 水手
vocabulary.context: The first successful mission to Venus was completed by NASA's Mariner 2 .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第一次成功的金星任务是由 NASA 的水手 2 号完成的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 好客的, 招待周到的
vocabulary.context: Even though Venus isn't the most hospitable planet in our solar system, it has certainly been a popular place to visit.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管金星不是太阳系中最适宜居住的行星，但它无疑是一个受欢迎的旅游胜地

vocabulary.definition: adj. 致命的；n. 致死因子
vocabulary.context: While Venus could earn the nickname of "most unwelcoming neighbor" due to its deadly air, crushing pressure, and lethal heat, this planet has always captivated humans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然金星因其致命的空气、巨大的压力和致命的热量而赢得了"最不受欢迎的邻居"的绰号，但这颗行星一直吸引着人类

vocabulary.definition: v. 创建理论, 建立学说, 推理( theorize的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: Outside of that, a lot of what scientists think about dreams is just theorizing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除此之外，科学家对梦的很多看法都只是理论上的

vocabulary.definition: n. 活化, 激活；[化] 活化; 活化作用; 激活; 激活作用
vocabulary.context: The "activation synthesis theory" says our dreams are random firings from memories, thoughts, and images within the brain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "激活合成理论"认为，我们的梦是大脑内记忆、思想和图像的随机激发

vocabulary.definition: n. 综合, 组织, 综合体；[化] 合成
vocabulary.context: The "activation synthesis theory" says our dreams are random firings from memories, thoughts, and images within the brain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "激活合成理论"认为，我们的梦是大脑内记忆、思想和图像的随机激发

vocabulary.definition: n. [希神][罗神]马头鱼尾的怪兽, [鱼]海马
vocabulary.context: As the mice slept, the scientists found bursts of electricity in two sections of the brain: the neocortex, which controls thoughts, and the hippocampus, which controls memory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当老鼠睡觉时，科学家们在大脑的两个部分发现了电流爆发：控制思想的新皮质和控制记忆的海马体

vocabulary.definition: n. 模拟, 假冒物, 模仿；[计] 模拟
vocabulary.context: This is called "threat simulation theory" and might explain why our dreams so often involve stressful events such as forgetting your homework.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这被称为"威胁模拟理论"，可以解释为什么我们的梦经常涉及压力事件，例如忘记做作业

vocabulary.definition: adj. 着重, 强调, 重读, 使受应力
vocabulary.context: This is called "threat simulation theory" and might explain why our dreams so often involve stressful events such as forgetting your homework.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这被称为"威胁模拟理论"，可以解释为什么我们的梦经常涉及压力事件，例如忘记做作业

vocabulary.definition: adv. 相像地, 类似于
vocabulary.context: Similarly, in the late 1600s, the philosopher and scientist René Descartes spent all day in a heated room, searching for the best way to come up with new knowledge.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 同样，在 1600 年代末，哲学家兼科学家勒内·笛卡尔 (René Descartes) 在一个暖气十足的房间里度过了一整天，寻找获得新知识的最佳方法

vocabulary.definition: n. 哲学家, 哲人, 思想开创人, 达观的人；[法] 哲学家, 哲学研究者
vocabulary.context: Similarly, in the late 1600s, the philosopher and scientist René Descartes spent all day in a heated room, searching for the best way to come up with new knowledge.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 同样，在 1600 年代末，哲学家兼科学家勒内·笛卡尔 (René Descartes) 在一个暖气十足的房间里度过了一整天，寻找获得新知识的最佳方法

vocabulary.definition: n. 原作者, 根源, 原创造者
vocabulary.context: The controversy around Shakespeare's true authorship first emerged in the 1800s.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 围绕莎士比亚的真实作者身份的争议首先出现在 1800 年代

vocabulary.definition: n. 德比(英国中部都市), 德比赛马, 大赛马
vocabulary.context: William Stanley, 6th Earl of Derby
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 威廉·斯坦利，第六代德比伯爵

vocabulary.definition: adj. 谦恭的, 礼让的, 有教养的
vocabulary.context: For authors of noble status, having their work appear in print instead of being restricted to private, courtly audiences would have led to disgrace.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于地位高贵的作者来说，让他们的作品出版而不是仅限于私人、宫廷读者可能会导致耻辱

vocabulary.definition: n. 平民, 自费学生, 有共用权的人；[法] 平民, 下议院议员, 有共有权的人
vocabulary.context: They would have had intimate knowledge of the court that a commoner would have lacked.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们对宫廷有着普通人所缺乏的深入了解

vocabulary.definition: n. 剧作家
vocabulary.context: Shakespeare was also inspired by various sources, as well as by his fellow dramatists.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莎士比亚也受到各种来源以及他的剧作家同行的启发

vocabulary.definition: n. 假名, 笔名；[法] 笔名, 假名
vocabulary.context: Supporters of this theory claim that "Shakespeare" was a pseudonym shared by many authors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该理论的支持者声称"莎士比亚"是许多作者共用的笔名

vocabulary.definition: n. 熏猪肉；[机] 腌肉
vocabulary.context: American writer Delia Bacon published a book in which she proposed that Shakespeare's works were not written by one person but by several.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 美国作家迪莉娅·培根出版了一本书，提出莎士比亚的作品不是一个人写的，而是几个人写的

vocabulary.definition: n. 贵族, 高尚, 贵族阶级
vocabulary.context: People who support the alternative authorship theory see this as proof that Shakespeare must have secretly been a nobleman since many of his plays feature nobility.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 支持另类作者理论的人认为这证明莎士比亚一定是秘密的贵族，因为他的许多戏剧都以贵族为特色

vocabulary.definition: n. 海贝壳
vocabulary.context: What do seashells, Renaissance painters, and sunflowers have in common?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 贝壳、文艺复兴时期的画家和向日葵有什么共同点？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 反时针方向的；adv. 反时针方向
vocabulary.context: He discovered, for example, that sunflower seeds radiate from the center of the flower in spirals, with a certain number of spirals going clockwise and a certain number of spirals going counterclockwise.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，他发现葵花籽从花的中心呈螺旋状辐射，其中一定数量的螺旋是顺时针方向的，一定数量的螺旋是逆时针方向的

vocabulary.definition: n. 众神庙, 万神殿, 罗马万神殿
vocabulary.context: Some people claim that the Pantheon in Rome, Notre Dame cathedral in Paris, and Taj Mahal in India all also use versions of the golden ratio.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有人声称罗马万神殿、巴黎圣母院、印度泰姬陵也都使用了黄金比例的版本

vocabulary.definition: n. 超现实主义者
vocabulary.context: The famous surrealist painter Salvador Dalí used the golden ratio in his masterpiece The Sacrament of the Last Supper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 著名超现实主义画家萨尔瓦多·达利在其代表作《最后的晚餐的圣礼》中运用了黄金比例

vocabulary.definition: adj. 连续的, 联贯的；[计] 连续的; 连接的
vocabulary.context: In Fibonacci's sequence, two consecutive numbers add up to the next number in the sequence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在斐波那契数列中，两个连续的数字相加为序列中的下一个数字

vocabulary.definition: adj. 和睦的, 调和的, 和谐的, 协调的
vocabulary.context: Rectangles in the golden ratio can be combined to form harmonious structures: both on paper or canvas and in real-life buildings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黄金比例的矩形可以组合形成和谐的结构：无论是在纸上或画布上还是在现实生活中的建筑物中

vocabulary.definition: n. 比, 比率；[医] 比, 比率, 比例
vocabulary.context: They all follow the golden ratio.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们都遵循黄金比例

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 排列, 排成一行, 结盟；v.t. 使结盟, 使成一行, 校正
vocabulary.context: Plants grow their leaves in spiral patterns that align to the golden ratio to ensure each leaf gets exposed to as much sunlight as possible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 植物以螺旋图案生长叶子，与黄金比例对齐，以确保每片叶子都暴露在尽可能多的阳光下

vocabulary.definition: adj. <美俚>傲慢的, 自负的, 趾高气扬的, 厚颜无耻的
vocabulary.context: Even as a child, her grandmother worried about her "talking biggety to white folks." Parks' life was defined by a deep personal belief in individual rebellion and self-preservation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使在她还是个孩子的时候，她的祖母就担心她"对白人说大话"。帕克斯的一生是由对个人叛逆和自我保护的深刻个人信念所定义的

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 反驳, 驳斥；驳倒
vocabulary.context: Instead, she is often thought of as a quiet old lady on the bus - an image she would later refute, saying: "People always say that I didn't give up my seat because I was tired, but that isn't true.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，她经常被认为是公交车上一位安静的老太太--她后来反驳了这一形象，她说："人们总是说我没有让座是因为我累了，但事实并非如此

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我保存；[法] 自卫, 自保, 自卫本能
vocabulary.context: Even as a child, her grandmother worried about her "talking biggety to white folks." Parks' life was defined by a deep personal belief in individual rebellion and self-preservation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使在她还是个孩子的时候，她的祖母就担心她"对白人说大话"。帕克斯的一生是由对个人叛逆和自我保护的深刻个人信念所定义的

vocabulary.definition: n. 自卫, 自卫术, 自卫权；[法] 自卫, 正当防卫
vocabulary.context: Parks was a firm believer in self-respect and self-defense.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 帕克斯坚信自尊和自卫

vocabulary.definition: n. 抢夺, 掠夺, 强奸, 葡萄渣, 芸苔；v.t. 掠夺, 抢夺, 强奸
vocabulary.context: She became involved in the civil rights movement while working with her husband to free the Scottsboro Boys, nine young black men accused of raping two white women.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她在与丈夫合作解救斯科茨伯勒男孩（Scottsboro Boys）时参与了民权运动，这九名年轻黑人被指控强奸了两名白人妇女

vocabulary.definition: n. 自尊, 自重；[法] 自尊
vocabulary.context: Parks was a firm believer in self-respect and self-defense.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 帕克斯坚信自尊和自卫

vocabulary.definition: n. 感性, 感觉, 情感；[医] 感觉性, 感受性; 感觉, 感觉能力
vocabulary.context: They found that the image of a quiet, tired old lady better fit the sensibilities of the country - which was a white majority.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们发现，一个安静、疲惫的老太太的形象更符合这个白人占多数的国家的情感

vocabulary.definition: n. 剩余物, 其他人, 残余, 余数；v. 削价出售(图书)；adj. 剩余的, 出售削价剩书的；[计] 余数
vocabulary.context: She spent the remainder of her life using her fame to inspire youth to make change and become advocates for justice and peace.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她在余生中利用自己的名气激励年轻人做出改变并成为正义与和平的倡导者

vocabulary.definition: v. 再点火, 再点燃, 重新激起( reignite的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: However, in 1956, his band performed at a Jazz Festival, which exposed him to a completely new generation of jazz fans and reignited his popularity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，1956年，他的乐队在爵士音乐节上演出，使他接触到了全新一代的爵士乐迷，并重新点燃了他的人气

vocabulary.definition: adj. 穿得很体面的
vocabulary.context: Ellington's casual manner, easy-going nature, and well-dressed appearance earned him the nickname "Duke" amongst his childhood friends.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 艾灵顿随意的举止、随和的性格以及衣着考究的外表为他赢得了儿时朋友们的绰号"公爵"

vocabulary.definition: n. 雷格泰姆旋律, 散拍乐；adj. 滑稽的
vocabulary.context: During high school, Ellington continued to take music lessons, and he imitated his favorite ragtime pianists.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 高中期间，艾灵顿继续上音乐课，他模仿他最喜欢的拉格泰姆钢琴家

vocabulary.definition: adj. 随和的
vocabulary.context: Ellington's casual manner, easy-going nature, and well-dressed appearance earned him the nickname "Duke" amongst his childhood friends.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 艾灵顿随意的举止、随和的性格以及衣着考究的外表为他赢得了儿时朋友们的绰号"公爵"

vocabulary.definition: v. 初次公演, 首次露面；n. 初次的演出；adj. 首次的, 首位的
vocabulary.context: He got to premiere one of them, called "Black, Brown, and Beige," at the prestigious Carnegie Hall, where he was invited to return for several years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他在著名的卡内基音乐厅首演了其中一部名为《黑色、棕色和米色》的作品，并多次受邀重返该音乐厅

vocabulary.definition: n. 夜总会
vocabulary.context: They played in Broadway nightclubs for several years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们在百老汇夜总会演出了几年

vocabulary.definition: adj. 淡棕色的；n. 薄斜纹呢, 米色
vocabulary.context: He got to premiere one of them, called "Black, Brown, and Beige," at the prestigious Carnegie Hall, where he was invited to return for several years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他在著名的卡内基音乐厅首演了其中一部名为《黑色、棕色和米色》的作品，并多次受邀重返该音乐厅

vocabulary.definition: n. 粘贴簿, 唱片套；[医] 白色物
vocabulary.context: His popularity continued to grow after his death, and people still buy his records and albums today.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他去世后，他的受欢迎程度继续增长，至今人们仍在购买他的唱片和专辑

vocabulary.definition: n. 舒适, 安逸, 畅快
vocabulary.context: Through the power of advertising, a scented candle becomes synonymous with the feeling of warmth, coziness, and being at home.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过广告的力量，香薰蜡烛成为温暖、舒适和宾至如归的感觉的代名词

vocabulary.definition: n. 染眉毛油；v.t. 在...上涂染眉毛油
vocabulary.context: even though the model looks that way because of Photoshop, not mascara.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使模型看起来是这样的，是因为 Photoshop，而不是睫毛膏

vocabulary.definition: n. 网页
vocabulary.context: An advertisement can be anything from the commercial before a YouTube video to the small banner on the side of a webpage.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 广告可以是任何内容，从 YouTube 视频之前的广告到网页侧面的小横幅

vocabulary.definition: n. 泔脚, 废话, 空洞的作品
vocabulary.context: It was generally thought that if a picture was trying too hard to sell you a product, the product was probably hogwash - absurd, foolish, and unlikely to work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人们普遍认为，如果一张图片过于努力地向你推销产品，那么该产品可能就是废话--荒谬、愚蠢，而且不太可能起作用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 提供消息的, 见闻广博的
vocabulary.context: Before the early 1900s, advertisements were more informative than persuasive, relegated to tiny print at the backs of magazines.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 1900 年代初之前，广告的信息性多于说服力，被降级为杂志背面的小印刷品

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无所不在的, 到处存在的, 普遍存在的
vocabulary.context: As ads become ubiquitous, these appeals to our desires can become harmful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着广告变得无处不在，这些对我们欲望的诉求可能会变得有害

vocabulary.definition: n. 整体, 积分, 整数；adj. 固有的, 整体的, 整数的, 积分的；[计] 积分
vocabulary.context: And a diamond ring, rare and valuable, becomes an integral part of getting engaged.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 稀有而珍贵的钻戒成为订婚不可或缺的一部分

vocabulary.definition: n. 组成, 性格, 化妆品, 化装(术), 虚构, 补考
vocabulary.context: A makeup company, like Maybelline, wants people to associate its product with feeling happy, healthy, skinny, and naturally beautiful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 像美宝莲这样的化妆品公司希望人们将其产品与快乐、健康、苗条和自然美丽联系起来

vocabulary.definition: n. 心电图；[计] 心电图
vocabulary.context: That reading is called an electrocardiogram, or ECG.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该读数称为心电图或 ECG

vocabulary.definition: adj. 两维的
vocabulary.context: He has used the same compare-and-match technique to teach a computer to turn a two-dimensional photo of a face into a three-dimensional one.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他使用相同的比较和匹配技术来教计算机将脸部的二维照片转换为三维照片

vocabulary.definition: adj. 指状的; 细长的
vocabulary.context: The scanner looks like a tall computer mouse with a finger-shaped pad on top.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 扫描仪看起来像一个高高的电脑鼠标，顶部有一个手指形状的垫子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 按部就班的
vocabulary.context: He creates step-by-step instructions, called algorithms, for a computer program to follow.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他创建了逐步的指令，称为算法，供计算机程序遵循

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可穿用的；n. 衣服
vocabulary.context: Martin says the goal is to create a single wearable item to replace all the smart cards, keys and passwords people now use.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马丁表示，我们的目标是创建一款可穿戴设备来取代人们现在使用的所有智能卡、钥匙和密码

vocabulary.definition: n. [计]由字母或数字组成的用户名称, 以标明用户的身份
vocabulary.context: The "username" for each medical file is the child's fingerprint.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每个医疗文件的"用户名"是孩子的指纹

vocabulary.definition: n. 生物统计学；[医] 生物统计学, 寿命预测(平均余命算定)
vocabulary.context: To do this, he and his team are using something called biometrics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为此，他和他的团队正在使用一种称为生物识别技术的技术

vocabulary.definition: adj. 眼睛的, 视觉的, 光学的；[机] 光学上的, 光学的
vocabulary.context: An optical physicist, Rowe studies light and how it interacts with things.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为一名光学物理学家，罗研究光及其与物体的相互作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 背教者, 变节者, 脱党者；adj. 背弃的, 脱离的, 叛徒的；v.i. 背叛, 背弃, 脱离
vocabulary.context: Now, you might play "Renegade" on repeat while your mom looks at you like, What is so good about this "go go go go" nonsense?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在，你可能会重复播放"Renegade"，而你妈妈会看着你，这种"走走走走"的废话有什么好处？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 发疯
vocabulary.context: Myths like these are completely bonkers!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 像这样的神话完全是疯狂的！

vocabulary.definition: n. 青春期, 春情发动期；[医] 青春期(12-17岁)
vocabulary.context: There are a bunch of silly myths about puberty - like eating too much greasy food causes acne, or shaving hair causes it to grow back thicker and darker.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 关于青春期有很多愚蠢的神话--比如吃太多油腻的食物会导致痤疮，或者剃掉头发会导致头发重新长得更粗更黑

vocabulary.definition: n. 除臭药, 防臭剂；adj. 止臭的, 防臭的
vocabulary.context: You might notice that you sweat a lot more than before (and if you don't notice, try not to be too embarrassed if one day your parent gives you some deodorant).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你可能会注意到你比以前出汗多了（如果你没有注意到，如果有一天你的父母给你一些除臭剂，尽量不要太尴尬）

vocabulary.definition: n. 喷射, 冲刺；v.t. 喷射；v.i. 喷出, 迸发, 冲刺
vocabulary.context: These changes do not happen all at once; in fact, one common change is a growth spurt, which can last for 2-3 years.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些变化不会一下子发生；事实上，一种常见的变化是井喷式增长，可持续 2-3 年

vocabulary.definition: n. 腋窝；[医] 腋, 腋窝
vocabulary.context: During puberty, you will start to grow armpit hair and possibly facial hair, too.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在青春期，您将开始长出腋毛，也可能长出面部毛发

vocabulary.definition: n. 成年；[医] 成人期
vocabulary.context: Adolescence is the time in your life when your body and mind start to mature in preparation for adulthood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 青春期是你一生中身体和思想开始成熟、为成年做准备的时期

vocabulary.definition: n. 逻辑, 逻辑学, 推理的方法, 推理, 逻辑性；[计] 逻辑
vocabulary.context: Your use of logic and problem-solving skills improves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 您的逻辑运用能力和解决问题的能力得到提高

vocabulary.definition: n. 头饰, 吊索

vocabulary.definition: adj. 盐的, 苦涩的, 由碱金属(或含镁之盐类)组成的；n. 盐湖, 盐田, 碱盐泻药, 生理盐水
vocabulary.context: He treats many patients by plugging up this cavity with saline (or salt water).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他通过用盐水（或盐水）堵塞这个空腔来治疗许多患者

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再生；重新长出；v.i. 重新生长

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 幸运的是，那些气味检测组织总是会重新生长

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他使用一种称为 PET（正电子发射断层扫描）的技术来观察六个人的大脑

vocabulary.definition: adj. 听觉的, 听觉器官的；[医] 听[音]的, 听觉的

vocabulary.definition: n. 压缩, 凝缩, 冷凝；[化] 冷凝; 冷凝作用; 缩合; 缩合作用; 凝聚; 凝聚作用; 凝结作用

vocabulary.context: Like a jigsaw puzzle, Earth's lithosphere is divided into interlocking pieces, called plates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就像拼图游戏一样，地球的岩石圈被分成互锁的部分，称为板块

vocabulary.definition: n. （非警方人员所做的）调查, 侦察
vocabulary.context: But it took some geological sleuthing to determine how and why.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但需要一些地质调查才能确定如何以及为何

vocabulary.definition: n. 漂积海草, 失事船只, 破坏；v.t. 严重伤害
vocabulary.context: The route is dotted by some serious volcanoes and wracked by violent earthquakes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这条路线上点缀着一些严重的火山，并遭受剧烈地震的破坏

vocabulary.definition: n. [德语](德国、奥地利、瑞典的)伯爵(相当于英国的earl)
vocabulary.context: Lucky for this team, 70 new seismographs (SIZE moh grafs) had recently been installed all over the world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于这个团队来说幸运的是，最近在世界各地安装了 70 台新地震仪（SIZE moh grafs）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未被证实的
vocabulary.context: In 1964, plate tectonics still was just an emerging - if unproven - concept.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1964 年，板块构造仍然只是一个新兴的概念（尽管尚未得到证实）

vocabulary.definition: n. 厚度, 密度, 愚钝, 含混不清；[医] 厚度
vocabulary.context: Their thickness ranges from 80 to 200 kilometers (50 to 124 miles).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它们的厚度范围为 80 至 200 公里（50 至 124 英里）

vocabulary.definition: adv. 决定性地, 最后地
vocabulary.context: It was only after the 1964 quake, he says, that scientists could definitively point to the effects of plate movements on Earth's surface.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他说，直到 1964 年地震之后，科学家们才能明确指出板块运动对地球表面的影响

vocabulary.definition: adj. 正面的
vocabulary.context: Along convergent boundaries, neighboring plates either collide head-on or a denser ocean plate dives beneath a lighter continental plate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 沿着会聚边界，相邻板块要么正面碰撞，要么密度较大的海洋板块潜入较轻的大陆板块下方

vocabulary.definition: n. 取消, 作废, 注销戳；[经] 注销, 取消, 保险单的注销
vocabulary.context: Another group of players faced a non-voluntary "course cancellation." Their players, too, got sent back to the beginning of the game - but there was nothing they could have done to prevent it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一组球员面临非自愿的"课程取消"。他们的球员也被送回了比赛开始的地方--但他们无法阻止这种情况发生

vocabulary.definition: adj. [解]腹正中的
vocabulary.context: That part is located right above the eyes and called the ventromedial prefrontal cortex (VEN-troh-MEED-ee-uhl Pree-FRON-tul KOR-tex).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该部分位于眼睛正上方，称为腹内侧前额皮质 (VEN-troh-MEED-ee-uhl Pree-FRON-tul KOR-tex)

vocabulary.definition: n. 减低, 减退, 松弛, 失望；[化] 调漆
vocabulary.context: No matter what causes a letdown, there can be many ways to cope, Bhanji explains.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 班吉解释说，无论什么原因导致失望，都有很多方法可以应对

vocabulary.definition: adj. 腹的, 腹部的, 腹面的；n. 腹鳍
vocabulary.context: For instance, the ventral striatum (VEN-truhl Stry-AY-tum) sits deep in the skull and is important in motivation - such as whether to try again.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，腹侧纹状体（VEN-truhl Stry-AY-tum）位于颅骨深处，对于动机很重要 - 例如是否再试一次

vocabulary.definition: n. 挫折, 退步, 逆流
vocabulary.context: After a setback we can't control, you realize that this "isn't due to your own actions [and] you can't correct that behavior," Bhanji explains.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在经历了我们无法控制的挫折之后，你意识到这"不是由于你自己的行为造成的，并且你无法纠正这种行为，"班吉解释道

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 战胜, 超越, 克服
vocabulary.context: This study helps scientists understand what helps people surmount setbacks, says Candace Raio.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Candace Raio 说，这项研究帮助科学家了解什么可以帮助人们克服挫折

vocabulary.definition: n. 强调, 加强, 重点, 强语气
vocabulary.context: And this is where successful people put more emphasis on interpreting their emotions in a way that allows them to forge ahead.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是成功人士更加注重以一种能够让他们不断前进的方式来诠释自己的情绪的地方

vocabulary.definition: n. 动机, 刺激, 推动；[医] 促动, 推动, 诱导
vocabulary.context: For instance, the ventral striatum (VEN-truhl Stry-AY-tum) sits deep in the skull and is important in motivation - such as whether to try again.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，腹侧纹状体（VEN-truhl Stry-AY-tum）位于颅骨深处，对于动机很重要 - 例如是否再试一次

vocabulary.definition: n. “嗜极生物”, 或称作“嗜极菌”, 生活在各种极端恶劣环境下的微生物。
vocabulary.context: "Makes me wish I were an extremophile like a tardigrade," says Edward Guinan.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "让我希望自己是一个像缓步动物一样的极端微生物，"爱德华·吉南说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 缓步的, 缓步类的；n. 缓步类动物, 节肢动物
vocabulary.context: Also known as tardigrades, these microscopic creatures are tough.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些微小的生物也被称为缓步动物，它们非常坚韧

vocabulary.definition: v. 突升, 猛涨( skyrocket的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: Temperatures plummeting to -272° Celsius (-460° Fahrenheit) or skyrocketing to 150 °C (300 °F)?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 温度骤降至 -272° 摄氏度（-460° 华氏度）还是飙升至 150 °C (300 °F)？

vocabulary.definition: n. 超新星
vocabulary.context: These explosions are thought to come from especially powerful supernovas or colliding stars.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些爆炸被认为来自特别强大的超新星或碰撞的恒星

vocabulary.definition: adj. 冷淡的, 不适于居住的, 不相容的
vocabulary.context: If these critters can live through such extreme conditions on our planet, it suggests life might exist in other seemingly inhospitable places.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果这些生物能够在我们星球上如此极端的条件下生存，这表明生命可能存在于其他看似荒凉的地方

vocabulary.definition: n. 启示, 天启
vocabulary.context: Until then, tardigrades can probably resist any other potential apocalypse. This is exciting to researchers who hope that if there's life elsewhere in the universe, it lasts long enough for us to find it. Notes Shostak, "Life is tough, once it gets going."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在那之前，缓步动物可能能够抵抗任何其他潜在的灾难。这让研究人员感到兴奋，他们希望如果宇宙其他地方存在生命，它的存在时间足以让我们找到它。肖斯塔克指出："生活一旦开始，就很艰难。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 难的, 艰苦的, 坚硬的, 勇敢的；adv. 努力地, 辛苦地, 坚硬地
vocabulary.context: And they are hardy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 而且他们很坚强

vocabulary.definition: n. 大破坏, 蹂躏；v.t. 严重破坏
vocabulary.context: Neither would global warming nor any astronomical events that wreak havoc on Earth's atmosphere.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 全球变暖或任何对地球大气层造成严重破坏的天文事件都不会

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 卷入, 吸进, 吞没, 使全神贯注
vocabulary.context: It might engulf Earth, and it will surely sizzle away Earth's water.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它可能会吞没地球，并且肯定会吸走地球上的水

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自我选择的
vocabulary.context: So these may have been a self-selected group of the most injured of players, not a representative mix of all players.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，这些可能是受伤最严重的球员自行选择的一组，而不是所有球员的代表性组合

vocabulary.definition: n. 神经病学者, 神经科医师；[医] 神经病学家
vocabulary.context: As a neurologist, he studies nerve tissues and the brain at the University of California, San Francisco.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为一名神经学家，他在旧金山加利福尼亚大学研究神经组织和大脑

vocabulary.definition: n. 脑病；[医] 脑病
vocabulary.context: Of them, 177 had CTE, short for chronic traumatic encephalopathy (En-sef-uh-LOP-ah-thee).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中，177 人患有 CTE，即慢性创伤性脑病 (En-sef-uh-LOP-ah-thee) 的缩写

vocabulary.definition: v. (使)集合, (使)聚集；adj. 集合在一起的, 集体的
vocabulary.context: Here, the protein congregates in cells around small blood vessels.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这里，蛋白质聚集在小血管周围的细胞中

vocabulary.definition: adj. 令人烦恼的, 麻烦的, 劳碌命的
vocabulary.context: Still, the study's authors find their new results worrisome.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不过，该研究的作者发现他们的新结果令人担忧

vocabulary.definition: n. 大猩猩, 残暴的男人, 歹徒
vocabulary.context: And that's "the 800-pound gorilla in the room," says neurologist David Brody.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 神经学家 David Brody 说，这就是"房间里的 800 磅重的大猩猩"

vocabulary.definition: n. 阻止, 妨碍, 预防；[医] 预防
vocabulary.context: For now, he says, "We need to focus on prevention of concussions and other head impacts at all levels of contact sports."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他说，目前，"我们需要重点关注预防各个级别的接触运动中的脑震荡和其他头部撞击。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 小心, 慎重, 警示；v.t. 警告；[计] 警告
vocabulary.context: The authors caution, however, that they don't know how representative the brains were that they studied.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，作者警告说，他们不知道他们研究的大脑有多大代表性

vocabulary.definition: n. 獠牙, 龅牙
vocabulary.context: Her heavy breathing and gaping bucktooth will be a sign she's deep in thought.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她沉重的呼吸声和张开的龅牙表明她正在沉思

vocabulary.definition: n. 迷迭香；[化] 迷迭香
vocabulary.context: She was by the rosemary bushes, playing with the carcasses of ladybugs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她在迷迭香丛旁，玩着瓢虫的尸体

vocabulary.definition: adj. 空谈的, 不实际的, 柏拉图哲学的, 柏拉图的
vocabulary.context: Said the relationship was too platonic.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 说这种关系过于柏拉图式

vocabulary.definition: n. 小长春花, 长春花, 玉黍螺
vocabulary.context: Try your best not to tense up if she questions why her room is not periwinkle anymore.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果她问为什么她的房间不再是长春花色，请尽量不要紧张

vocabulary.definition: n. 下颌的轮廓,下巴的外形
vocabulary.context: Tell yourself that it's Alice who has her cheekbones and Derrick who has her jawline.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 告诉自己，爱丽丝有她的颧骨，德里克有她的下巴

vocabulary.definition: n. 九十几, 九十年代( ninety的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: Touch the spaces on your wall where there were once posters of classic movies from the nineties.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 触摸墙上曾经贴有九十年代经典电影海报的空间

vocabulary.definition: n. 起重机, 小偷；[化] 吊杆起重机; 人字起重机
vocabulary.context: She told you Derrick needed them for good luck on his test.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她告诉你德里克需要它们来保证考试好运

vocabulary.definition: n. 共同统治, 共管下的政府, 共管, 多层公寓大楼, 由个人占用的一套公寓房间
vocabulary.context: The condo will smell like paint and plywood, but by now you're used to it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 公寓闻起来像油漆和胶合板的味道，但现在你已经习惯了

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 攻其不备
vocabulary.context: "And I was just blindsided by this.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我对此感到措手不及

vocabulary.definition: n. 遁辞, 借口, 托辞；[法] 遁词, 托词, 藉口
vocabulary.context: In her later races, no subterfuge was necessary.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在她后来的比赛中，不需要任何诡计

vocabulary.definition: adj. 结实的, 粗壮的, 率直的
vocabulary.context: You know, we laugh about it now because it's so funny when a girl is saved by her burly boyfriend.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你知道，我们现在会嘲笑这件事，因为当一个女孩被她魁梧的男友救下时，这真是太有趣了

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使活跃, 激励, 供给...能量；[电] 激励
vocabulary.context: Switzer's coach in 1967 was a 15-time Boston Marathoner and didn't think a woman could do it - which energized Switzer to try.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1967 年，Switzer 的教练曾 15 次参加波士顿马拉松比赛，他认为女性不可能做到这一点 - 这激励了 Switzer 去尝试

vocabulary.definition: adj. 喜不自禁的, 欢闹的, 引人发笑的
vocabulary.context: You're going to grow hair on your chest.' It was hilarious, the myths.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你的胸口将会长出毛发。"这很搞笑，神话

vocabulary.definition: n. 马拉松, 耐力的考验
vocabulary.context: Still, despite proof that women could clearly complete marathons, the athletic world generally assumed that women "couldn't run and didn't want to run" that far, Switzer says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯维策表示，尽管有证据表明女性显然可以完成马拉松，但体育界普遍认为女性"不能跑也不想跑"那么远

vocabulary.definition: n. 竞争者；[经] 竞争者, 竞争对手
vocabulary.context: As the years went on she advocated for women to be admitted as full competitors - and kept running more and more marathons.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着时间的推移，她倡导女性被接纳为正式参赛者，并继续参加越来越多的马拉松比赛

vocabulary.definition: n. 大头针, 针, 别针, 栓, 销子, 图钉, 插头, 管脚, 品(液量单位)；v.t. 将...用针别住, 钉住, 压住, 牵制, 使不能动, 归罪于；adj. 针的, 销子的, 闩的
vocabulary.context: At the point where she was once confronted by that race official, she posted a Facebook Live video - smiling as she ran, with her bib number, 261, pinned safely in place.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当她遇到那位比赛官员时，她发布了一段 Facebook 直播视频--边跑边微笑，她的号码布号码 261 被安全地固定在适当的位置

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 假定, 承担, 呈现；v.i. 装腔作势, 僭越
vocabulary.context: Still, despite proof that women could clearly complete marathons, the athletic world generally assumed that women "couldn't run and didn't want to run" that far, Switzer says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯维策表示，尽管有证据表明女性显然可以完成马拉松，但体育界普遍认为女性"不能跑也不想跑"那么远

vocabulary.definition: adj. 半圆的；[医] 半环形的
vocabulary.context: There, a semicircular area is enclosed by a wall, which bears the names of the 19 people who were hanged.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在那里，有一个半圆形的区域被墙围起来，上面刻着19名被绞死的人的名字

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不正当地, 不妥地
vocabulary.context: The memorial was primarily funded with a $174,000 Community Preservation Act grant, though the city adds that it also received "dozens of small donations, many from descendants of those wrongfully executed at the site."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该纪念馆主要由 174,000 美元的《社区保护法》拨款资助，不过该市补充说，它还收到了"数十笔小额捐款，其中许多来自在该地点被错误处决的人的后代"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不义地, 不法地
vocabulary.context: "We should not be here commemorating the heartbreaking and tragic loss of life, people who were falsely and unjustly accused of being in the snare of the devil."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们不应该在这里纪念令人心碎和悲惨的生命损失，那些被错误和不公正地指控陷入魔鬼网罗的人们。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 极小之物, 针尖, 立锥之地, 精确位置；v.t. 精确地找到, 准确地轰炸, 刺穿, 准确描述, 使突出, 强调；adj. 针尖的, 极微的, 精确的, 详尽的, 精确定位的
vocabulary.context: Shea says that researchers pinpointed the location of the site in January 2016.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Shea 表示，研究人员在 2016 年 1 月确定了该地点的位置

vocabulary.definition: n. 赎回, 赎身, 偿还, 拯救；[经] 偿还, 买回, 赎回
vocabulary.context: "In that sense, it really is our national story, our national shame and our national chance at redemption because you know if you're not a descendant of one of those people probably the person standing next to you is," Baker told Shea.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "从这个意义上说，这确实是我们国家的故事、我们国家的耻辱和我们国家救赎的机会，因为你知道，如果你不是这些人的后裔，那么站在你旁边的人很可能就是，"贝克告诉谢伊

vocabulary.definition: adv. 虚伪地, 错误地, 不实地
vocabulary.context: "We should not be here commemorating the heartbreaking and tragic loss of life, people who were falsely and unjustly accused of being in the snare of the devil."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们不应该在这里纪念令人心碎和悲惨的生命损失，那些被错误和不公正地指控陷入魔鬼网罗的人们。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 陷阱, 诱惑, 圈套；v.t. 以陷阱捕获, 诱惑
vocabulary.context: "We should not be here commemorating the heartbreaking and tragic loss of life, people who were falsely and unjustly accused of being in the snare of the devil."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们不应该在这里纪念令人心碎和悲惨的生命损失，那些被错误和不公正地指控陷入魔鬼网罗的人们。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 纪念
vocabulary.context: "We should not be here commemorating the heartbreaking and tragic loss of life, people who were falsely and unjustly accused of being in the snare of the devil."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们不应该在这里纪念令人心碎和悲惨的生命损失，那些被错误和不公正地指控陷入魔鬼网罗的人们。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 执行, 实行, 完成, 处死, 制成；[计] 执行
vocabulary.context: The memorial was primarily funded with a $174,000 Community Preservation Act grant, though the city adds that it also received "dozens of small donations, many from descendants of those wrongfully executed at the site."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该纪念馆主要由 174,000 美元的《社区保护法》拨款资助，不过该市补充说，它还收到了"数十笔小额捐款，其中许多来自在该地点被错误处决的人的后代"

vocabulary.definition: n. 乐队彩车, 流行
vocabulary.context: Other networks jumped on the reality show bandwagon.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他网络也加入了真人秀节目的行列

vocabulary.definition: n. 庞然大物
vocabulary.context: But within a couple of years, MTV had grown into a behemoth of the music industry.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但几年之内，MTV 已成长为音乐行业的庞然大物

vocabulary.definition: adj. 机敏的, 猜疑的, 狡猾的
vocabulary.context: Jackson led to Lionel Richie, Billy Ocean and other black musicians, though leery network executives had to be persuaded all over again when rap became popular.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 杰克逊引领了莱昂内尔·里奇、比利·奥恩和其他黑人音乐家，尽管当说唱流行时，必须再次说服心存疑虑的网络高管

vocabulary.definition: adj. 纯白的, 无疵的, 纯真的
vocabulary.context: Thriller caused a controversy at lily-white MTV.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 《Thriller》在 lily-white MTV 引起争议

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 编译, 编辑, 编纂, 收集；[计] 编译
vocabulary.context: Craig Marks and Rob Tannenbaum have compiled a new oral history of the network, I Want My MTV: The Uncensored Story of the Music Video Revolution .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Craig Marks 和 Rob Tannenbaum 编写了新的网络口述历史，《我想要我的 MTV：音乐视频革命的未经审查的故事》

vocabulary.definition: n. 魔鬼, 邪神, 能手, 成癖者
vocabulary.context: "They thought that MTV was a bunch of coked-up rock and roll fiends, and they were right in a way," Marks says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他们认为 MTV 是一群喝醉了的摇滚乐恶魔，他们在某种程度上是对的，"马克斯说

vocabulary.definition: n. 酒鬼, 酒精中毒者；adj. 酒精的
vocabulary.context: "It's an alcoholic, crooked line all the way there, but MTV quickly realized and learned that narrative television, even reality TV, rated better than music videos."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这是一条酒精性的、弯曲的路线，但 MTV 很快意识到并了解到叙事电视，甚至真人秀，比音乐视频收视率更高。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 传说的, 传奇中说的, 传奇般的, 传说中的, 著名的
vocabulary.context: The company was just a few bad months away from going under, until several ad executives cooked up what became a legendary ad campaign featuring rock stars yelling, "I want my MTV!"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 几个月后，该公司就濒临破产，直到几位广告高管策划了一场传奇的广告活动，其中摇滚明星大喊："我想要我的 MTV！"

vocabulary.definition: n. 勤劳, 工业, 企业, 产业, 有组织的劳动；[经] 工业, 实业
vocabulary.context: But within a couple of years, MTV had grown into a behemoth of the music industry.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但几年之内，MTV 已成长为音乐行业的庞然大物
